title: Tak Sakaguchi Joins Iko Uwais in ‘Pendekar: Warrior’ as Nemesis (EXCLUSIVE)
full_text: Jul 22, 2026 3:58am PT Tak Sakaguchi Joins Iko Uwais in ‘Pendekar: Warrior’ as Nemesis (EXCLUSIVE) By Naman Ramachandran Plus Icon Naman Ramachandran Latest Han Chae Young Scores a Hit With FlareFlow Korean Remake of Global Microdrama ‘Raising His Mistress’s Child’ 3 hours ago Rebel Wilson Wins Defamation Case Against Charlotte MacInnes Over ‘The Deb’ Bath Dispute 4 hours ago FAST Provider Wedotv Launches in India With Samsung TV Plus (EXCLUSIVE) 1 day ago See All Asia Media Alliance Group Japanese cult action star Tak Sakaguchi has signed on to play the nemesis of Iko Uwais in “ Pendekar: Warrior ,” an upcoming martial-arts feature marking Sakaguchi’s first major screen role outside his home country. Principal photography is scheduled to begin in the fourth quarter of 2026. Sakaguchi, known to action devotees for his cult roles in “Versus” and “Re:Born,” has built his reputation on an unrelenting physical approach to screen fighting. He is widely associated with one of the more extraordinary sequences in action cinema: a 77-minute single-take fight in “Crazy Samurai Musashi” in which he faces down some 400 opponents. His pairing with Uwais – internationally recognized for “The Raid,” “The Raid 2,” “Headshot,” “The Night Comes for Us,” “Mile 22” and “Expend4bles” – brings together two performers from rival martial-arts traditions who have not previously shared a screen. Related Stories John Lasker, a Top ESPN Streaming Executive, to Exit in September ESPN Plunges Back Into Shohei Ohtani Money Transfer Scandal With New Tisha Thompson Podcast “Iko and Tak share something incredibly rare: when they fight on screen, the audience feels that every movement has purpose and consequence,” director, filmmaker and cinematographer John Radel ACS said. “They come from different martial-arts traditions, but they have the same uncompromising commitment to truth in performance. Watching these two finally face one another will be visceral, emotional and unlike anything audiences have seen from either of them before.” Popular on Variety Set on the streets of Jakarta, “Pendekar: Warrior” centers on a former silat master who leaves prison intent on putting violence behind him, only to find a crime syndicate has taken hold of his old neighborhood. Forced to fight again to protect those closest to him, the character becomes the vehicle through which the film aims to bring Indonesian silat to a global audience. Both Uwais and Sakaguchi are in active physical preparation, working with the production’s action team to develop sequences that blend silat’s speed with Sakaguchi’s close-quarters style. “This is more than a clash between two great action performers,” Radel added. “We are creating action that comes from character and emotion. When Iko and Tak finally collide, it has to feel like two unstoppable forces meeting, and like a moment action fans have been waiting years to witness.” The film is produced by Asia Media Alliance Group alongside leading Indonesian producer Delon Tio of Nation Pictures, Erika North and Nick North of Yeti Entertainment, and veteran action producer Mike Leeder of Hong Kong-based Red Packet Media. Uwais and Ryan Santoso are attached as executive producers through Uwais Pictures, with K-Movie Entertainment serving as co-producer and international sales agent. “Bringing Iko Uwais and Tak Sakaguchi together is a landmark moment for Asian action cinema,” Tio said. “They are two extraordinary performers who have each built a global following through authenticity, discipline and fearless physical storytelling. ‘Pendekar: Warrior’ represents exactly the kind of bold, internationally focused Indonesian film we want to bring to audiences around the world.” “I’ve been a huge fan of Tak and his work since the early days, going right back to ‘Versus,'” Leeder said. “I’ve always wanted the chance to work with him, and now we get to pit his close-quarters combat against Iko’s silat in one hell of a movie.” Uwais is represented by Brett Norensberg at Gersh and Vault Entertainment. ‘The Wolf King’ Romantic Fantasy Books in the Works as a Starz Series ‘S.W.A.T’ Spinoff ‘Exiles’ Lands at Starz, Sets September Release Date ‘Power: Legacy’ Starring Joseph Sikora, Michael Rainey Jr. Greenlit at Starz ‘Power Book III: Raising Kanan’ Introduces Young [SPOILER], Character to Appear in ‘Power: Origins’ Longtime Disney Exec Jim Kapenstein, Who Helped Oversee Some of Its Biggest Acquisitions, Joins Starz as Chief Legal and Strategy Officer Starz to Develop ‘Bone Parish’ Series Executive Produced by Curtis ’50 Cent’ Jackson Loading comments... title: Catherine Zeta-Jones Revenge Thriller ‘Kill Jackie’ Acquired by AMC in the U.S.
full_text: Jul 22, 2026 3:33am PT Catherine Zeta-Jones Revenge Thriller ‘Kill Jackie’ Acquired by AMC in the U.S. By Ellise Shafer Plus Icon Ellise Shafer Latest Catherine Zeta-Jones Revenge Thriller ‘Kill Jackie’ Acquired by AMC in the U.S. 32 minutes ago Jacob Elordi Spent Two Days ‘Locked in a Coffin’ With a Real Snake for ‘Euphoria’ Death: Sam Levinson ‘Brought Me Back to Torture Me’ 18 hours ago ‘Godzilla vs. Kong’ Star Kaylee Hottle Dies at 18 20 hours ago See All Catherine Zeta-Jones in 'Kill Jackie' (Credit: Unai Mateo) AMC Global Media has acquired the U.S. rights to Catherine Zeta-Jones ‘ upcoming revenge thriller series “ Kill Jackie .” Starring and executive produced by Zeta-Jones and co-produced by Steel Springs Pictures and Fremantle, “Kill Jackie” is set to premiere this fall on AMC+ in the U.S. Based on Nick Harkaway’s bestselling novel “The Price You Pay” (which he wrote under pseudonym Aidan Truhen), the series follows Jackie Price (Zeta-Jones), “who has spent the last 20 years living a quiet, luxurious existence selling fine art while remaining anonymous after escaping a criminal past,” according to its official synopsis. “Her carefully constructed world is shattered when she discovers that The Seven Demons, a squad of ruthless assassins, have been hired to kill her. Jackie will need to unleash her old instincts and execute a dangerous plan to take down the Demons.” Related Stories ESPN Plunges Back Into Shohei Ohtani Money Transfer Scandal With New Tisha Thompson Podcast John Lasker, a Top ESPN Streaming Executive, to Exit in September Alongside Zeta-Jones, “Kill Jackie” stars Daniel Ings, Sidse Babett Knudsen, Bill Paterson, Ron Perlman, Óscar Jaenada, Hattie Hook, Darci Shaw, Raff Law, Enzo Cilenti, Christine Adams, Julian Rhind-Tutt, Karlis Arnolds Avots, Set Sjöstrand, Tadashi Ito, Sebastian Armesto, Julian Barratt, Gavin Spokes, Jonathan Cake and Bamshad Abedi-Amin. Popular on Variety “’Kill Jackie’ is a standout addition to AMC+’s curated slate of premium scripted content, and a great example of the bold, character-driven storytelling for which we are known,” Dan McDermott, chief content officer of AMC Global Media and president of AMC Studios, said in a statement. “Catherine Zeta-Jones commands the screen with a magnetic leading performance, and we’re thrilled to be working with her and the entire producing team to bring this daring, dark, comedic thriller to U.S. audiences.” Added Peter Lawson, Steel Springs Pictures founder: “We’re thrilled to partner with AMC Global Media to bring ‘Kill Jackie’ to audiences in the U.S. AMC has built an exceptional reputation for championing bold, premium storytelling, making it the ideal home for this series. We couldn’t be more excited for viewers to experience ‘Kill Jackie’ on AMC+.” “Kill Jackie” is co-created by Conor Keane (“North Sea Connection”), Tom Butterworth (“Gangs of London”), Peter Lawson (“John Wick”) and Damon Thomas (“Killing Eve”). Alongside Zeta-Jones, executive producers include Lawson and Jose Agustin Valdes for Steel Springs Pictures as well as Butterworth adn Thomas with Rebecca Dundon, Dante Di Loreto and Jeffrey Levine. Lawson and Lorenzo De Maio of De Maio Entertainment negotiated the deal and co-manage U.S. sales. Fremantle handles sales in all other territories. title: European Film Academy CEO and Greek New Wave Director Join Emily Watson on Sarajevo Jury
full_text: × Skip to main content By Lily Ford Plus Icon Lily Ford @lilyfordtweets Follow Sign Up View All July 22, 2026 3:13am (L-R) Matthijs Wouter Knol, Athina Rachel Tsangari, Igor Bezinović, Sara Klimoska. Courtesy Sarajevo Film Festival Director of the European Film Academy Matthijs Wouter Knol and Yorgos Lanthimos’ producer Athina Rachel Tsangari are among the industry professionals tapped to join British star Emily Watson on the Sarajevo Film Festival jury. Alongside filmmaker Igor Bezinović and actress Sara Klimoska, they will judge the feature film competition at the Bosnian film festival, running for a week in mid-August. Watson joins a rich constellation of auteurs who have previously presided over the Sarajevo jury in past editions, including Mike Leigh, Jeremy Irons, Asghar Farhadi, Ruben Östlund, and Mia Wasikowska. Related Stories Movies 'The Brutalist' EP, 'Nawi' Makers Strike Three-Film Co-Pro Deal, Set Kenya Soccer Doc 'Champions of Nowhere' (Exclusive) Movies Nicolas Winding Refn Reveals the Chaotic Making of 'Drive': "Most Everyone Hated It" The group will award the Heart of Sarajevo Awards in the feature film competition at the ceremony on Aug. 21. Watson most recently starred as Mary Shakespeare in Chloé Zhao’s adaptation of Maggie O’Farrell’s award-winning historical novel Hamnet . Her notable film credits include Lars von Trier’s Breaking the Waves , A24’s God’s Creatures opposite Paul Mescal, Small Things Like These , Netflix drama Steve , and the Oscar-winning Gosford Park . On television, Watson currently leads the HBO/Max series Dune: Prophecy and has just finished shooting the second season. Croatian director Bezinović is best known for his 2025 film Fiume o morte! , which won numerous awards, including the European Film Award for best documentary, as well as AFA Awards (Adriatic Film & TV Awards) for best film, best director, and best screenplay. His other works include the documentaries The Blockade (2012) and Veruda – A Film About Bojan (2015), both of which received the Oktavijan Award for best Croatian documentary film. His debut feature, A Brief Excursion (2017), premiered at Rotterdam and went on to win the Grand Golden Arena for best film at the Pula Film Festival. Klimoska is an actress from North Macedonia whose latest leading role is in Sister Brother Manhole Cover , directed by Milcho Manchevski, which is set to premiere in 2026. This marks her third collaboration with the acclaimed director, following Willow and Kaymak . Her credits also include You Won’t Be Alone by Goran Stolevski, which premiered at Sundance, and Housekeeping for Beginners , which screened at the Venice Film Festival. Internationally, she has appeared in Nico by Eline Gehring, Family Secrets by Malik Vitthal, and Replay by Jason Lester. Klimoska has been part of the Talents Sarajevo program at Sarajevo Film Festival, and the corresponding scheme at the Berlinale. In 2022, she was nominated for the Heart of Sarajevo award for her role in the TV series Underneath . Knol is an industry-renowned film producer, lecturer, editor and journalist from the Netherlands. Since 2021, he has been the CEO and director of the European Film Academy. He is responsible for the executive management, the vision and all related programming of current and future activities of the Academy, including brand and audience development, knowledge sharing and film heritage projects, and the annual European Film Awards. Knol has been part of the main jury at the festivals of Buenos Aires, Jerusalem, Istanbul, and Locarno. Greek director-screenwriter-producer Tsangari is considered a principal instigator of the Greek New Wave. Her genre-bending films explore power and desire in gender, class and alterity. Her debut project, The Slow Business of Going (2001), was a sci-fi road movie shot in hotel rooms across nine cities around the globe. It went on to garner several best film and best director awards worldwide, and now resides in MoMA’s permanent film collection. Her sophomore feature Attenberg (2010) premiered at Venice in competition, where it won the Coppa Volpi and the Lina Mangiacapre Directing award. Chevalier (2015) won best film at the BFI London Film Festival and was nominated for best foreign film at the Independent Spirit Awards. Both movies were Greece’s official submissions for best international film at the Oscars. Under the banner of her Athens-based company Haos Film, she has produced a number of award-winning films such as Yorgos Lanthimos’ Kinetta and Alps , Georgis Grigorakis’ Digger , and was co-producer on Richard Linklater’s Before Midnight . Her latest feature, Harvest , a neo-western starring Caleb Landry Jones and Harry Melling, was shot in the Scottish Highlands and premiered at Venice in 2024. THR

title: Guillermo del Toro, Kim Novak, Ari Aster Lined Up for ‘The Fall,’ Investigating Hitchcock’s ‘Biggest Mystery’ – the Stunning ‘Vertigo’ Finale (EXCLUSIVE)
full_text: Jul 22, 2026 3:11am PT Guillermo del Toro, Kim Novak, Ari Aster Lined Up for ‘The Fall,’ Investigating Hitchcock’s ‘Biggest Mystery’ – the Stunning ‘Vertigo’ Finale (EXCLUSIVE) Alexandre O. Philippe’s documentary two-parter is set to be unveiled July 22 at Fantasia’s Frontières By Jennie Punter Plus Icon Jennie Punter Latest Psychological Horror Feature ‘Ancestral Beasts’ Debuts Trailer Ahead of Fantasia World Premiere (EXCLUSIVE) 22 hours ago ‘The Plague’ Exec Producer James Presson Boards Frontières Biopic Parody ‘Humpty: American Dream’ (EXCLUSIVE) 1 week ago Frontières Juicy Dark Horror Comedy ‘Gro(ceries),’ Starring and Co-Written by ‘Sex Education’s’ Chinenye Ezeudu-Sterling, Adds French Co-Production Partners (EXCLUSIVE) 1 week ago See All 'The Fall' (working title) writer-director Alexandre O. Philippe, courtesy of the production Guillermo del Toro, Kim Novak, and Ari Aster are among several star “witnesses” already lined up for Alexandre O. Philippe ’s two-parter doc feature “The Fall,” which is set to be unveiled July 22 at Fantasia’s Frontières as part of its International Co-Production Market pitch event. A two-part film, “The Fall” will be produced by Medianoche Productions, behind Philippe’s 2025 Venice-premiering doc “ Kim Novak’s Vertigo .” Medianoche producer Terri Piñon and writer-director Philippe are reunited with France’s Tell Me Films, their producing partner on the Novak film. The partners are also teamed up with France’s Thunderbug Productions for the new title. Related Stories Sundance Film Festival, International Federation of Film Critics Establish Jury to Assess Movies From First-Time Foreign Directors (EXCLUSIVE) With a pre-sale to France’s Cine+ also locked, Piñon and Philippe land at Frontières looking for an international sales agent and financing partners for a project that is not only in production – with roughly a year to go – but also gathering momentum as it moves towards a milestone plot point in cinema history. Popular on Variety “We’re building ‘The Fall’ as a two-part event, with separate premieres for Part I and Part II, for ‘Vertigo’s’ 70th anniversary in 2028 that will be the cinephile event of the year,” Piñon said. “With the cast we’re securing, the French pre-sale, the co-production structure, and early interest from some of the most prestigious festivals, it’s set up for a spectacular rollout to ride the ‘Vertigo’ anniversary buzz throughout 2028.” The working title “The Fall” refers to the iconic and fascinatingly ambiguous finale of Hitchcock’s “Vertigo.” When Judy plummets to her death from the belltower, the viewer is never shown exactly how it happens, and instead is shown 57 frames of Scottie’s face as he watches. The film is structured as a film-noir investigation of the fall and the emotions and ideas it inspires. Philippe, whose 2024 Venice Classics Lion winner “Chain Reactions” explores the impact of Tobe Hooper’s “The Texas Chain Saw Massacre” 50 years after its release, said he wants to “inhabit” rather than solve the riddle of the fall. Each interview subject offers “a different version of the truth,” said Philippe, who has called te “Vertigo” finale Hitchcock’s ‘biggest mystery.’ “Each version reveals something intimate about the person telling it. It’s the culmination of everything I’ve chased for 20 years: the film I was born to make.” Fantasia’s four-day Frontières Market runs July 22-25 in Montreal. title: Venice Giornate Degli Autori Unveils 2026 Selection Dedicated To Late Founder Giorgio Gosetti
full_text: Skip to main content By Melanie Goodfellow Melanie Goodfellow Senior International Film Correspondent @mel_goodfellow More Stories By Melanie Edward Stourton Dies: UK Broadcaster Whose Most Recent Work Charted 250 Years Of American History Was 68 Venice Critics’ Week Unveils 2026 Selection With Films From Colombia, China & Tunisia Mia Threapleton, Danny Huston Join Guy Pearce, Jared Harris & Merab Ninidze In ‘The Price Of Peace’ As Filming Begins in London View All July 22, 2026 3:05am My Notes On Mars GdA Venice parallel section Giornate degli Autori (GdA) has unveiled a female focused lineup for its 2026 edition. Hungarian director Lili Horvát’s first English-language film My Notes on Mars will open the edition. The sci-fi romance stars Mackenzie Davis as a scientist who vanishes during a hiking trip to reappear on the day of her own memorial service with no memory of the life she has lived in between. Rupert Friend co-stars. Horvát previously made waves with Preparations to Be Together for an Unknown Period of Time , about a talented surgeon who ditches her career for a man who then claims to have never met her before. That film debuted in the GdA’s 2020 edition and then went on to win numerous awards including a Golden Hugo in Chicago’s New Directors Competition. Related Stories Festivals Venice Critics' Week Unveils 2026 Selection With Films From Colombia, China & Tunisia News Giovanni Veronesi's 'Dio Ride' To Close Venice Film Festival Léa Domenach’s A Man’s Skin , described as a queer and feminist musical comedy set in the Italian Renaissance, and featuring Pomme, Karin Viard, and Catherine Deneuve in the cast, will close the section. Watch on Deadline Drawn from 1,036 submissions from 114 countries, the lineup features 25 world premieres, of which 13 are Italian and 14 directed by women; with 10 films in competition, one closing film out of competition, five special events, and a showcase of nine titles in Venetian Nights (scroll down for the full section). Horvát’s My Notes on Mars is among 10 films being showcased in competition. Further contenders include Polish-born Australia-based filmmaker Kuba Dorabialski’s Agata the Writer , starring Mia Wasikowska as an Australian writer who travels to Sarajevo to work on a novel about the Bosnian war and is forced to acknowledge the limits of her own artistic vision. In other competition highlights include Post Tenebras Lux actress Nathalia Acevedo’s directorial debut Salón de Belleza . Adapted from the radical novel by Mario Bellatin, it unfolds in a beauty salon in a city ravaged by a mysterious disease which becomes a refuge for the afflicted as they linger between life and death. Belgian multi-disciplinary artist Sam Louwyck, and Erwan Kepoa Falé co-star. It is among five first features in the mix alongside Italian director Nicola Sorcinelli’s Balcanica , about father and son musicians fleeing Afghanistan; Ukrainian filmmaker Mykyta Gibalenko’s Camouflage about a young man dealing with Russia’s invasion of his homeland of Ukraine as he tries to build a new life in Germany; Swiss artists Pauline Julier and Nicolas Chapoulier’s 1600s-set tale The Indies , about a mission to transport Velázquez’s portrait of the Infanta of Spain from Madrid to Versailles as a peace offering, as well as I Plant My Hand in the Garden , a docu-fiction by Iranian director Hoda Taheri and the queer Serbian director Boris Hadžija tackling issues at the heart of their identities and lives. Further contenders include Nigerian director Damilola Orimogunje’s second film Dear Ajayi , taking its cue from the complicated relationship between two sisters and the burden of expectations that their families and society place on them, and Argentinian director María Aparicio’s third feature Sadness Sometimes, Other Times Revolt , revolving around the encounters between two Argentinians, a director and a historian, living in Madrid. Established directors in the mix include Robert Guédiguian with A Woman Today . Newcomer Marilou Aussilloux stars as a young woman grappling with the distance between her own idea of freedom and the contradictions of a present day still ridden with violence and inequality, alongside the director’s longtime collaborators Ariane Ascaride, Jean-Pierre Darroussin, and Gérard Meylan. This year’s jury for the GdA Director’s Award, which includes a cash prize of €20,000 to be split between the director and international distributor, comprises Spanish filmmaker Carla Simón, Italian director and editor Sara Fgaier, and Hania Mroué, director of Beirut’s landmark Metropolis arthouse cinema. In other awards, audiences for the GdA screenings at the Sala Perla will decide the Audience Award while the Europa Cinemas jury will choose the winner of the label from among the European films at Giornate degli Autori. The Special Events lineup – featuring the documentary, autobiography, historical film, and experimentation, includes Francesca Archibugi’s Carla Lonzi, A Feminist Stor y, exploring the life and times of one of the most radical feminist voices of 20th-century Italy. In other highlights beyond the main competition, Claire Denis and Mona Fastvold are the guests of honor of the Miu Miu Women’s Tales program. The 15th edition of the initiative in partnership with GdA will see the filmmakers share career lessons in on-stage conversations. GdA’s 23rd edition is dedicated to the sidebar’s founder and longtime champion, film critic and festival exec Giorgio Gosetti who died suddenly and unexpectedly in March. “This edition carries with it a deep void and a deep gratitude,” declares Artistic Director Gaia Furrer. “We owe to Giorgio the very idea for this free arena we inhabit, along with his faith in the new directions of cinema and his stubborn, loving determination to build and consolidate, year after year, a community of men and women filmmakers and audiences. This year, Giornate springs from the desire to honor this legacy by continuing to do what Giorgio taught us: seek out, discover, go wrong, become passionate about, and believe in cinema and in those who make it happen.” The edition, will run alongside the Venice Film Festival from September 2 to 12, with its activities based mainly in the Casa degli Autori and the Sala Laguna on the Venice Lido. OFFICIAL SELECTION IN COMPETITION SADNESS SOMETIMES, OTHER TIMES REVOLT by María Aparicio Argentina, Spain, 2026 AGATA THE WRITER by Kuba Dorabialski Australia, 2026 BALCANICA – debut feature by Nicola Sorcinelli Italy, Serbia, Croatia, 2026 CAMOUFLAGE – debut feature by Mykyta Gibalenko Germany, 2026 DEAR AJAYI by Damilola Orimogunje Nigeria, Germany, 2026, 120’ I PLANT MY HAND IN THE GARDEN – debut feature by Boris Hadžija & Hoda Taheri Germany, Serbia, U.S. The Indies – debut feature by Pauline Julier, Nicolas Chapoulier Switzerland, Spain, 2026 MY NOTES ON MARS – opening film by Lili Horvát Hungary, Austria SALÓN DE BELLEZA – debut feature by Nathalia Acevedo Mexico, France, 2026 A WOMAN TODAY by Robert Guédiguian France, Italy, 2026 OUT OF COMPETITION MAN’S SKIN – closing film by Léa Domenach France, 2026 SPECIAL EVENTS AL BARANEYA by Ashgan El-Hamus Netherlands, Egypt, Belgium, Saudi Arabia, 2026 CARLA LONZI, A FEMINIST STORY by Francesca Archibugi Italy, 2026 THE PHANTOM by Ignacio Masllorens, David Lamelas Argentina, 2026 THE MASSACRE by Cosimo Messeri Italy, 2026 EVEN IF HELL CAME by Samuele Rossi Italy, 2026 Why Lisa Nandy Could Become The Most Important UK Politico In The U.S. Huge $20M Tuesday; 2nd U.S. Frame Could Top Nolan’s ‘Dark Knight’ Pics Sony Looking To Bring Cinerama Dome & Hollywood Arclight Back To Life Disney Cutting Several Hundred Jobs At Pixar, ESPN, Nat Geo & Other Units Read More About: Comments

title: Mia Wasikowska, Catherine Deneuve, Rupert Friend in 2026 Venice Days
full_text: × Skip to main content By Scott Roxborough Plus Icon Scott Roxborough Europe Bureau Chief sroxborough Follow Sign Up View All July 22, 2026 3:00am 'Agata the Writer' Courtesy of Venice Days Venice Days , the independent sidebar of the Venice International Film Festival , has unveiled the lineup for its 23rd edition, the first since the death in March of section founder and longtime head Giorgio Gosetti. This year’s Venice Days, running Sept. 2-12 alongside the 83rd Venice Festival, is dedicated to his memory. The sidebar, and its accompanying Italian cinema section Venice Nights, includes 25 world premieres, more than half of which (14) are directed by women. My Notes on Mars from Hungarian director Lili Horvát ( Preparations to Be Together for an Unknown Period of Time ) will open Venice Days, screening in competition. Horvát’s English-language debut stars Mackenzie Davis and Rupert Friend in the story of a young scientist who mysteriously disappears only to show up weeks later at her own memorial service with no memory of what happened. Related Stories TV After German Broadcaster Yanks "Antifascist" Song, Musicians Give Guerrilla YouTube Concert News Rebel Wilson Cleared in 'The Deb' Defamation Case Kuba Dorabialski’s Agata the Writer , starring Mia Wasikowska as an Australian author in Sarajevo, will also premiere in competition, alongside Pauline Julier and Nicolas Chapoulier’s period drama The Indies ; Boris Hadžija and Hoda Taheri’s hybrid fiction-documentary I Plant My Hand in the Garden ; and Nicola Sorcinelli’s debut Balcanica , the only Italian title in competition, about an Afghan father and son who, after music is banned in their country, try to flee to Europe. ‘Camouflage’ Courtesy of Venice Days Other highlights include María Aparicio’s Sadness Sometimes, Other Times Revolt , a drama centered on two Argentines in Madrid; Mykyta Gibalenko’s debut Camouflage , about a young Ukrainian in Germany whose separation from home is transformed by Russia’s invasion of Ukraine; a Damilola Orimogunje’s family melodrama Dear Ajayi , which will be the first Nigerian movie to screen in Venice Days competition. Léa Domenach’s Man’s Skin , a queer feminist musical comedy set in the Italian Renaissance and featuring Catherine Deneuve, will close Venice Days, screening out of competition. ‘Man’s Skin’ Laurent-Champoussin An all-woman jury, headed by Spanish filmmaker Carla Simón ( Alcarràs , Summer 1993 ) with Italian director Sara Fgaier ( Weightless ), and Hania Mroué, director of Beirut’s Metropolis Cinema, will pick the winner of the GdA Director’s Award, which carries a €20,000 ($23,000) cash prize to be split between the director and the film’s international distributor. The Lido audience will vote for the Audience Award, while the Europa Cinemas jury will choose the Europa Cinemas Label from the European films in the lineup. Roberta Torre’s Scarpe rotte will open Venetian Nights, the sidebar’s Italian cinema program. The film, starring Barbara Ronchi, is freely inspired by the life of Natalia Ginzburg and is set in wartime Rome. Miu Miu Women’s Tales, a short film anthology series created by fashion brand Miu Miu, returns to Venice Days with Discipline , a new short from Mona Fastvold ( The Testament of Ann Lee ) and Avec Kim , from French auteur Claire Denis ( Beau Travail ), the latter starring Suzanne Lindon and Kim Gordon. Mona Fastvold’s short film ‘Discipline’ Courtesy of Venice Days The 2026 Venice Days will also include talks, industry events and public programs at the Casa degli Autori and Sala Laguna. Among them are the return of #confronti, the fourth edition of the Declaration of Filmmakers, a soundtracks event organized with ACMF, the second edition of Africa Chiama, and a new collaboration with Venezia CULT that will bring cartoonists Leo Ortolani and Dave McKean to the Lido. The Venice Film Festival will announce its competition lineup on Thursday, July 23. The full 2026 Venice Days lineup: IN COMPETITION Sadness Sometimes, Other Times Revolt , dir: María Aparicio (Argentina, Spain) Agata the Writer , dir: Kuba Dorabialski (Australia) Balcanica , dir: Nicola Sorcinelli (Italy, Serbia, Croatia) Camouflage , dir: Mykyta Gibalenko (Germany) Dear Ajayi , dir: Damilola Orimogunje (Nigeria, Germany) I Plant My Hand in the Garden , dirs.: Boris Hadžija, Hoda Taheri (Germany, Serbia, USA) The Indies , dirs.: Pauline Julier, Nicolas Chapoulier (Switzerland, Spain) My Notes on Mars , dir: Lili Horvát (Hungary, Austria) Salón de Belleza , dir: Nathalia Acevedo (Mexico, France) A Woman Today , dir: Robert Guédiguian (France, Italy) OUT OF COMPETITION Man’s Skin , dir: Léa Domenach (France) Closing Film SPECIAL EVENTS Al Baraneya , dir: Ashgan El-Hamus (Netherlands, Egypt, Belgium, Saudi Arabia) Carla Lonzi, A Feminist Story , dir: Francesca Archibugi (Italy) The Phantom , dirs.: Ignacio Masllorens, David Lamelas (Argentina) The Massacre , dir: Cosimo Messeri (Italy) Even If Hell Came , dir: Samuele Rossi (Italy) VENICE NIGHTS Anatomia di un ritratto , dirs.: Francesco Clerici, Mattia Colombo (Italy, Switzerland) Auber 89 , dir: Giuseppe Schillaci (France, Italy) Domenica capitale – Genere domenicale , dir: Giovanna Giuliani (Italy) La leggenda del deserto , dir: Elisabetta Giannini (Italy) Memoria del lampo , dir: Riccardo Giacconi (Italy) Il mestiere , dir: Beppe Tufarulo (Italy, Belgium, France) Scarpe rotte , dir: Roberta Torre (Italy) Opening Film Si sveglia e sbadiglia, il gatto; poi l’amore , dirs.: Federico Francioni, Samuele Sestieri (Italy) Uno si distrae al bivio , dir: Alessandra Lancellotti (Italy) THR

title: Venice Days Unveils Female-Centric Selection, Kicking Off With Lili Horvát’s ‘My Notes on Mars’ Starring Mackenzie Davis and Rupert Friend
full_text: Jul 22, 2026 3:00am PT Venice Days Unveils Female-Centric Selection, Kicking Off With Lili Horvát’s ‘My Notes on Mars’ Starring Mackenzie Davis and Rupert Friend The independently-run section's all-female jury is headed by Catalan director Carla Simón By Nick Vivarelli Plus Icon Nick Vivarelli International Correspondent @NickVivarelli Latest Venice Days Unveils Female-Centric Selection, Kicking Off With Lili Horvát’s ‘My Notes on Mars’ Starring Mackenzie Davis and Rupert Friend 55 minutes ago FIFA World Cup and Olympic Ceremonies Producer Balich Wonder Studio Expands Footprint to North America and Mexico 21 hours ago World Cup Final Ratings Skyrocket Across Europe, Despite England and France Losses 2 days ago See All Courtesy Giornate degli Autori Hungarian filmmaker Lili Horvát’s first English-language feature, “My Notes on Mars,” starring Mackenzie Davis and Rupert Friend, will open the Venice Film Festival ’s independently run Giornate Degli Autori . The section has unveiled a richly assorted lineup featuring works by female directors and exploring the female universe, regardless of who is behind the camera. This years’s selection of the Giornate – which is also known as Venice Days – comprises 25 world premieres across several sections, 14 of which are directed by women. The hotly anticipated “My Notes on Mars” by Horvát — whose last film “Preparations to Be Together for an Unknown Period of Time” debuted at Venice and Toronto in 2020 — centers on an astrophysicist with a troubled marriage named Margot who vanishes while hiking with her husband, Sam. Weeks later, she suddenly reappears on the day of her own memorial service. Margot remembers nothing about her life — except her Mars research. Popular on Variety “Notes on Mars” is among 10 Venice Days competition entries that will be judged by an all-female jury headed by Spanish director Carla Simón (“Alcarràs,” “Romeria”). The other jurors are Hania Mroue, founder and director of Beirut’s beloved Metropolis Cinema, and Italian director and producer Sara Fgaier. Polish-born director Kuba Dorabialski’s “Agata the Writerm” starring Mia Wasikowska as a Polish-Australian satirical novelist who travels from Sydney to Sarajevo to write a novel about the Bosnian war only to be challenged by locals, is also ensconced in a competition berth. Other standout Venice Days competition titles include Italian director Nicola Sorcinelli’s “Balcanica,” about Afghan musicians traveling along the Balcan route after music has been banned in their country, starring Matilda De Angelis (“Citadel: Diana”) who is also a co-producer on the film; Nigerian director Damilola Orimogunje’s “Dear Ajayi,” about two estranged Nigerian sisters who reunite at the wake of a tragedy; and French filmmaker Robert Guèdiguian’s “A Woman Today,” about a young woman named Juliette who holds a top job in finance and whose world collapses when she is assaulted by a colleague at a company event. Special events include Italian director Francesca Archibugi’s doc “Carla Lonzi, dentro e fuori dal mondo,” about radical feminist activist and art critic Carla Lonzi; Dutch-Egyptian director Ashgan El-Hamus’ debut “Al Baraneya,” about a young, pregnant, woman in an Egyptian village who is mulling the prospect of a promising future in the city if their family land is sold; and the closing film, which is French director Lèa Domenach’s queer and feminist musical comedy “A Man’s Skin,” which is set in the Italian Renaissance and re-teams the director with Catherine Deneuve after their collaboration on Bernadette Chirac comedy “The President’s Wife.” Shorts by Mona Fastvold and Claire Denis will unspool as part of the Prada-commissioned Miu Miu Women’s Tales, a series of short films directed by women. In a statement, the section’s artistic director Gaia Furrer pointed out the variety of narrative forms on display. She described them as: “sentimental science fiction, experimental documentaries, history films, family melodramas, anti-hero westerns, musical comedies and other works that freely cross the borders between documentary film and fiction, autobiography and invention.” “It’s a patchwork of genres that reflects the vibrancy of contemporary cinema and its ability to evolve along with the world itself,” Furrer said. This edition of Venice Days is dedicated to the memory of Italian film critic Giorgio Gosetti, who in 2004 founded the Venice Days section – which is modeled on Cannes’ Directors’ Fortnight and supported by Italian film directors’ associations ANAC and 100autori. Gosetti died in March in Rome at 70 years old. “For 23 years, without interruption, he was its [Venice Days’] guiding spirit, the lifeblood of the organization, motivating and uniting the professionals that turned it into a festival that is known and respected the world over,” a statement noted. The 23nd edition of Venice Days will run Sept. 2-12, in tandem with the official Venice Film Festival selection. See the full lineup below. IN COMPETITION “Sadness Sometimes, Other Times Revolt,” Maria Aparicio “Agata the Writer,” Kuba Dorabialski “Balcanica,” Nicola Sorcinelli “Camouflage,” Mykyta Gibalenko “Dear Ajayi,” Damilola Orimogunje “I Plant My Hand in the Garden,” Boris Hadžija and Hoda Taheri “Les Indes,” Pauline Julier, Nicolas Chapoulier “My Notes on Mars,” Lili Horvàt – Opening Film “Salón De Bellezza” Nathalia Acevedo “A Woman Today,” Robert Guédiguian OUT OF COMPETITION “Man’s Skin,” Léa Domenach SPECIAL EVENTS “Al Baraneya,” Ashgan El-Hamus “Carla Lonzi, A Feminist Story,” Francesca Archibugi “The Phantom,” Ignacio Masilorens, David Lamelas “The Massacre,” Cosimo Messeri “Even if Hell Came,” Samuele Rossi WOMEN’S TALES PROJECT (shorts), in collaboration with Prada’s Miu Miu Label “Discipline,” Mona Fastvold “Avec Kim,” Claire Denis ‘S.W.A.T’ Spinoff ‘Exiles’ Lands at Starz, Sets September Release Date ‘Power Book III: Raising Kanan’ Introduces Young [SPOILER], Character to Appear in ‘Power: Origins’ ‘Power: Legacy’ Starring Joseph Sikora, Michael Rainey Jr. Greenlit at Starz Longtime Disney Exec Jim Kapenstein, Who Helped Oversee Some of Its Biggest Acquisitions, Joins Starz as Chief Legal and Strategy Officer ‘The Wolf King’ Romantic Fantasy Books in the Works as a Starz Series Alliance of Women Directors and Starz Reveal Creative Leadership Lab’s Inaugural Class Designed to Support Female TV Directors (EXCLUSIVE) Loading comments... title: Elon Musk Says Grok Will Make Feature-Length, AI-Generated and “Historically Accurate” ‘The Odyssey’ Movie Before the Year Ends
full_text: × Skip to main content By Lily Ford Plus Icon Lily Ford @lilyfordtweets Follow Sign Up View All July 22, 2026 2:55am Jimmy Gonzales, Matt Damon, Himesh Patel in 'The Odyssey.' Melinda Sue Gordon/© Universal Pictures/Courtesy Everett Collection And so Elon Musk ‘s anti- Odyssey campaign continues: The X owner and recently-anointed trillionaire has vowed that his AI social media bot, Grok, will make “a full-length movie of The Odyssey that is historically accurate and true to the art of Homer” before the end of the year. His post on Wednesday was accompanied by a three-minute AI-generated short that a follower had made using Grok Imagine. It comes as the South African businessman continues to rile up his supporters in a real culture war over Christopher Nolan ‘s box office behemoth. His commentary on the film dates to January, when rumors of Lupita Nyong’o’s casting as Helen of Troy first began circulating. He has criticized Nolan and Hollywood for their “woke” agenda, despite wide acknowledgement from actual historians that Homer’s work is, of course, a piece of fiction. Related Stories Movies How Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' Conquered the Box Office Movies Matt Damon Reveals Why He Had Female Stunt Double for 'Odyssey' Scene: "Greatest Arms I've Ever Seen" Before this year ends, Grok Imagine will make a full-length movie of The Odyssey that is historically accurate and true to the art of Homer https://t.co/bVHzUmY9WN — Elon Musk (@elonmusk) July 22, 2026 In May, Musk agreed with a racist comment made by a far-right journalist who took aim at the Oscar-winning actress’ involvement: “We’re told that we shouldn’t object to Helen of Troy being portrayed as a black woman,” said Daily Wire ‘s Matt Walsh. “And yet if a major Hollywood studio made a film set in Africa and cast a white woman as ‘the most beautiful woman in Africa,’ those same people would literally riot in the street. If, say, Sydney Sweeney was cast in the role, they’d be driven to murderous violence. We all know this is the case.” Musk chimed in via the comment section of Walsh’s post: “Absolutely true. Such hypocrisy in Hollywood.” It isn’t the only cast announcement that the Tesla and SpaceX founder has taken issue with. When rumors surfaced that trans actor Elliot Page might be cast as Achilles, he reacted even more forcefully, saying: “One of the dumbest and most twisted things I’ve ever heard.” (Musk, who has a trans daughter from his first marriage, Vivian Jenna Wilson, has historically made transphobic attacks against his own daughter. She has cut ties with him.) It’s also worth noting that Page does not play Achilles in Nolan’s film — he portrays Sinon, a Greek soldier incorporated into the story from Virgil’s epic poem The Aeneid . So, it isn’t entirely surprising Musk would attempt to make his own Odyssey with what will be — judging from the short snippet he reposted on Wednesday — an all-white cast. Grok Imagine’s retelling of the Homer epic will likely only make Nolan’s feat look even more of a masterpiece, especially since it was shot entirely on Imax film and used extremely little CGI. Nolan himself has called the backlash over historical accuracy “irrelevant.” He said: “Comes with the territory… These conversations that happen before people see the film — they’re always irrelevant, because no one having them knows what the film actually is yet.” And for all of the outrage Musk has drummed up around The Odyssey , the film has not notably suffered at the box office at all. The retelling of classical Greek mythology got the box office back on track over the weekend, scoring $124.5 million on 3,900 North American screens. Earning an A CinemaScore from audiences, the film sailed away to 2026’s biggest live-action opening, with only Toy Story 5 ($159 million) and The Super Mario Galaxy Movie ($131 million) debuting above it. The Odyssey also claimed the top R-rated opening of the year and Universal’s highest weekend launch ever for an R-rated movie. Globally, The Odyssey collected $264.1 million, adding $139.6 million from 73 international markets. The film marks Nolan’s biggest worldwide opening of all time, topping The Dark Knight Rises’ $249 million global launch in 2012. THR

title: Top Trumps Panel Show, New Seasons of ‘Mock the Week’ Unveiled as Part of Warner Bros. Discovery U.K.’s TLC Comedy Slate
full_text: Jul 22, 2026 2:40am PT Top Trumps Panel Show, New Seasons of ‘Mock the Week’ Unveiled as Part of Warner Bros. Discovery U.K.’s TLC Comedy Slate By K.J. Yossman Plus Icon K.J. Yossman @KJYossman Latest Top Trumps Panel Show, New Seasons of ‘Mock the Week’ Unveiled as Part of Warner Bros. Discovery U.K.’s TLC Comedy Slate 1 hour ago Lisa Nandy Stays in Post as U.K. Culture Secretary, Kanishka Narayan Appointed First AI Minister 1 day ago World Cup Final Ratings Skyrocket Across Europe, Despite England and France Losses 2 days ago See All Courtesy of TLC TLC in the U.K. has ordered a Top Trumps-themed panel show set to be hosted by comedian Katherine Ryan. Based on the card game and tentatively titled “Top Trumps: The Comedy Pack,” it will see Ryan in charge of proceedings as team captains Joel Dommett and Jon Richardson battle it out with classic card face-offs, live studio challenges and bluffing games. It is produced by TalkbackThames with Naked and set to launch early next year. “Everyone knows Top Trumps, but no one has ever played it quite like this,” said Ryan. “We’re taking this iconic game and supersizing it into something bigger, louder and much funnier. And with Jon and Joel captaining rival teams, chaos is pretty much guaranteed.” Popular on Variety The news comes as part of a bumper comedy slate for the newly free-to-air channel, which is owned by Warner Bros. Discovery U.K. & Ireland. It includes a three-season order of “ Mock the Week ,” which will take the series up to Season 5 in 2027. The long-running panel show, which went on hiatus in 2022 after two decades at the BBC, was revived for the network earlier this year and has already broadcast Season 1 plus a summer special (Season 2 is set to air on Sept 13). It is hosted by Dara Ó Briain and produced by Angst productions. The network has made a concerted effort to invest in comedy since re-launching at the beginning of this year. As well as “Mock the Week,” TLC debuted another new format, “Unacceptable,” hosted by Ed Gamble which launched last month and has already been renewed for a second season. “Six months on from TLC’s successful launch as a free-to-air, broad entertainment channel, it’s hugely exciting to bring another brand-new original format to the schedule in ‘Top Trumps: The Comedy Pack’ (w/t),” said Graham Lafferty, WBD’s SVP for content strategy and networks in the U.K. and Ireland. “We are thrilled to have the brilliantly funny Katherine Ryan at the helm, no stranger to TLC after appearances on ‘Mock the Week’ and ‘Unacceptable,’ along with an exceptional comedy pairing in Joel Dommett and Jon Richardson. Our take on Top Trumps is perfectly placed for TLC’s growing U.K. comedy line-up, alongside ‘Mock the Week’ – which we are delighted to secure for a further three series with Dara Ó Briain satirically steering us through the 2027 news agenda – and a second series of ‘Unacceptable’ hosted by the hilarious Ed Gamble.” WBD is also investing in its motoring content with “The Race Mechanic,” “Richard Hammond’s Workshop” and “Wheeler Dealers World Tour,” among other titles set to land on TLC’s sister network Quest. ‘Outlander: Blood of My Blood’ Season 2 Sets Starz Release Date, Drops Tearjerking First Teaser Starz Acquires Russell T Davies’s Drama ‘Tip Toe’ for the U.S. and Canada Alliance of Women Directors and Starz Reveal Creative Leadership Lab’s Inaugural Class Designed to Support Female TV Directors (EXCLUSIVE) Longtime Disney Exec Jim Kapenstein, Who Helped Oversee Some of Its Biggest Acquisitions, Joins Starz as Chief Legal and Strategy Officer ‘S.W.A.T’ Spinoff ‘Exiles’ Lands at Starz, Sets September Release Date ‘Power Book III: Raising Kanan’ Introduces Young [SPOILER], Character to Appear in ‘Power: Origins’ Loading comments... title: After German Broadcaster Yanks “Antifascist” Song, Musicians Give Guerrilla YouTube Concert
full_text: × Skip to main content By Scott Roxborough Plus Icon Scott Roxborough Europe Bureau Chief sroxborough Follow Sign Up View All July 22, 2026 2:31am Pianist Igor Levit and singer Danger Dan at a 2022 concert in Berlin. Carsten Koall/picture alliance via Getty Images Let’s talk about fascism, baby. That was the message from German musicians Danger Dan and Igor Levit at a benefit concert in Berlin Tuesday night. The duo, known for their political activism, were set to perform their new “antifascist” song “Keine Angst” (No Fear), on Die Anstalt , a popular German topical comedy show. But the network, public broadcaster ZDF , pulled them. The channel said the song could be considered an incitement to violence and was not in line with their program guidelines. Related Stories News Rebel Wilson Cleared in 'The Deb' Defamation Case Movies Snoop Dogg Biopic Lands Summer 2027 Release From Universal So the pair put on a concert at Berlin’s Columbia theater, and live-streamed it on YouTube. Instead of talking about being canceled by ZDF, and the fierce debate it has sparked in the German media about freedom of speech, the band focused on raising the alarm about the rise of Germany ‘s far-right. “How should we deal with the fascists who are entering parliaments and taking to the streets?” Danger Dan asked the Berlin audience. “How do we deal with right-wing violence that is exploding and becoming increasingly brutal?” “How do we help people [who] fear leaving their homes because Nazis are patrolling?” added Levit. “How can one alleviate their fear and show them they are not alone?” ZDF director General Norbert Himmler defended the decision to pull the band. Their new song is “a manual for violent left-wing resistance,” Himmler told German newspaper Süddeutschen Zeitung . The lyrics to “Keine Angst” include lines suggesting activists out “nazi pigs” by doxxing far-right extremists. The song ends with a what appears to be a friendly greeting to left-wing activists who have been either convicted of, or are on trial for, violent attacks against members of the far-right. But not everyone agrees with ZDF’s decision. The hosts of Die Anstalt called the network’s decision “cowardly,” and used their show to argue that ZDF should have let Levit and Danger Dan perform. The comedians attended Tuesday’s concert, sitting in the front row. In an open letter on Monday, eight members of ZDF’s television council called out Himmler for pulling the song. “ZDF missed an opportunity to spark a debate about right-wing extremism in Germany and the various ways to combat it,” the letter reads. “The cancellation of the performance by Danger Dan and Igor Levit thus demonstrates not only a lack of courage but also a lack of trust in the program’s editorial team…We fear this could have a chilling effect.” The debate is coming against the backdrop of rising support for the far-right Alternative for Germany (AfD), a xenophobic and anti-migrant party that is currently leading national opinion polls. Many consider the AfD a threat to German democracy and have called for the party to be banned. The party is not currently in power anywhere in the country but that could change this year. The AfD is within striking distance of achieving an absolute majority in state elections in Saxony-Anhalt in September. THR

title: Analysis: As The New UK Prime Minister Cosies Up To Donald Trump, Lisa Nandy Could Be About To Become The Most Consequential Culture Secretary In America In Recent Memory
full_text: Skip to main content By Max Goldbart Max Goldbart International TV Co-Editor @Goldbart1 More Stories By Max Analysis: As The New UK Prime Minister Cosies Up To Donald Trump, Lisa Nandy Could Be About To Become The Most Consequential Culture Secretary In America In Recent Memory Apple TV Seeking Local Language Boss Catherine Zeta-Jones Thriller ‘Kill Jackie’ Sells To AMC+ View All July 22, 2026 2:24am Lisa Nandy speaks onstage during the Greater Together Los Angeles welcome reception at the Academy Museum of Motion Pictures in L.A. Jerod Harris/Getty for Greater Together Los Angeles UK Culture Secretary Lisa Nandy was widely expected to change jobs when her good friend Andy Burnham became Prime Minister this week, but she has remained in post and is now comfortably the longest-serving in that role for well over 10 years. Now, a set of decisions that Nandy is due to make over the coming months could make her one of the most consequential Culture Secretaries in recent memory… in America that is. This trio of major calls that loom over Nandy could end up upsetting or possibly ingratiating virtually all the major U.S. studios, streamers and tech giants, coming at a time when Burnham will be attempting to keep relations with Donald Trump rosy after the solid work put in by his predecessor Keir Starmer. Related Stories Ratings Netflix's 'Little House On The Prairie' Gathers Steam In Second Week As 'The Hawk' Opens Under Par Ratings Michael B. Jordan Animated Pic 'Swapped' Breaks Into Netflix's Most Popular Movies List First up, it was just before UK Parliament broke for recess and just before Burnham became PM that Nandy left the Paramount -Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) $110B mega-merger unread. Watch on Deadline She had initially said she was “minded to intervene” in the deal on media plurality grounds but failed to provide an update in time. David Ellison and co will now have to wait until September 1 at the very earliest to discover if the deal will be either probed or greenlit by British regulators. This, as a reminder, could be an incredibly costly Nandy intervention, because the agreement has a “ticking fee” commitment to WBD shareholders of 25 cents a ​share — roughly $650 million — for every quarter its takeover does not finalize beyond Q3. Ellison attempted to charm Nandy back in Jan during a much-publicized trip to Europe but any charm will dissipate if she causes his beloved deal to run into the “ticking fee” period. In the U.S., of course, he continues to hit stumbling blocks and the merger is currently on pause in response to a lawsuit from a dozen states. With the Paramount-WBD deal firmly remaining in the spotlight, WBD’s previous suitor Netflix will be fuming if Nandy pushes on with a controversial plan for the BBC licence fee to expand to subscribers to streamers like Netflix , Disney+ and Prime Video. The BBC has floated the plan in a desperate bid to generate more from the £180 ($240) annual fee. It has repeatedly stressed that while 94% of the UK population use its services every month, fewer than 80% pay the £180, leading to a loss of hundreds of millions of pounds per year. Without a great deal of info about how it would actually work in practice, Nandy recently signaled that she is behind the proposal that would see people who watch non-live content on streamers forced to pay the licence fee, and would even task streamers with helping collect the charge. The Motion Picture Association, which represents big streamers and studios, had already pushed back , saying the plan will have a “downstream impact on viewers and their viewing experience,” yet this doesn’t appear to have halted Nandy. She says she has had conversations with the streamers over various ideas and continues to point out that the government will never bring in a streamer levy, an idea taken up by several other nations that would force the SVoDs to pay a small amount of their UK subscription revenue to a cultural fund for British content. Netflix in particular is already on the European regulatory warpath. It has bristled frequently at other nations that force the streamer into local content quotas and funding obligations but has regularly praised the UK for leaving it free to do what it wants in the nation that birthed Adolescence and Baby Reindeer. Forcing its subscribers into effectively funding a rival would surely go against this grain. And then there is the tech giants. In amongst everything else, the UK is going ahead with a full social media ban for under-16s while also drawing up legislation that would require YouTube and TikTok to carry public service content prominently, which could prove quite the algorithmic headache for these tech behemoths. This idea was first floated at the RTS Convention by, you guessed it, Lisa Nandy, who said it will be “vital in the fierce battle against mis and disinformation.” Had Nandy been removed from her post earlier this week, it is impossible to tell if her successor would have changed tack on any of these three crucial upcoming decisions. But as it stands, she appears keen to follow through, and the American entertainment and tech giants that would be impacted would be numerous. Many of these companies have got closer and closer to Trump during his second presidency, some relying on his or his allies’ say-so to force through various deals. In their first-ever call during his first few hours as PM, Burnham invited Trump to his home city of Manchester, which also happens to be where Nandy is from. Were Trump to take up this offer, the Culture Sec may fancy making herself scarce. title: Elon Musk Says Grok Will Make an AI ‘Odyssey’ Film That Is ‘Historically Accurate’ by the End of the Year
full_text: Jul 22, 2026 2:07am PT Elon Musk Says Grok Will Make an AI ‘Odyssey’ Film That Is ‘Historically Accurate’ by the End of the Year By Alex Ritman Plus Icon Alex Ritman @alexritman Latest ‘Spider-Man: Brand New Day’ Final Trailer Unveils Action-Packed New Footage of Mark Ruffalo’s Hulk Battling the Web-Slinger 19 hours ago ‘Jason Statham Stole My Bike’ Action-Comedy Adds Eddie Peng, Daniel Ings and Artie Wilkinson-Hunt to Cast (EXCLUSIVE) 20 hours ago ‘Sunny Dancer’ Starring Bella Ramsey Bought by Quiver, Plans North American Theatrical Release (EXCLUSIVE) 22 hours ago See All Getty Images/Everett Collection Elon Musk has — finally — broken his silence on “ The Odyssey ” (well, at least since Christopher Nolan’s epic was released and started breaking various box office records). The tech mogul and recently-crowned trillionaire — who spent some time before the film’s launch rallying against various casting decisions — wrote on his social media platform X that his AI tool Grok Imagine would make a “historically accurate” full-length feature version of Homer’s ancient Greek poem. He noted that his AI “The Odyssey” would be ready by the end of 2026 (just in time for awards season). Related Stories ESPY Awards 2026 Livestream: Here's How to Watch the Sports Awards Ceremony Online John Lasker, a Top ESPN Streaming Executive, to Exit in September “Before this year ends, Grok Imagine will make a full-length movie of The Odyssey that is historically accurate and true to the art of Homer,” he posted, together with an AI-generated 3-minute short of “The Odyssey” posted by an X user. Popular on Variety Before this year ends, Grok Imagine will make a full-length movie of The Odyssey that is historically accurate and true to the art of Homer https://t.co/bVHzUmY9WN — Elon Musk (@elonmusk) July 22, 2026 Given the presence of cyclops, six-headed monsters and a witch who turns sailors into pigs in “The Odyssey,” a nearly 2,800 year old epic considered to be a work of fiction, how “historically accurate” any retelling — AI generated or otherwise — can be is likely to be called into question. Presumably, Musk is referring to the racial element, with him having been the most prominent voice attacking the casting of Lupita Nyong’o as Helen of Troy. In March he wrote that “Chris Nolan is an anti-White racist,” claiming that he only cast Nyong’o because “He wants the awards.” While not confirmed, the all-white AI cast of the short film he posted on X would appear to be more in line with what Musk would prefer. Musk had also supporting transphobic posts about Elliot Page, who many initially asserted was playing Achilles. Page was later confirmed as playing Sinon. For all Musk’s complaints about “The Odyssey” before its release, the movie has been nothing short of a triumphant success for Universal and appears destined to become Nolan’s highest-grossing film to date. The film earned a massive $264 million in its worldwide opening weekend and looks set to continue its own box office odyssey into the coming weeks. title: Apple TV Seeking Local Language Boss
full_text: Skip to main content By Max Goldbart Max Goldbart International TV Co-Editor @Goldbart1 More Stories By Max Analysis: As The New UK Prime Minister Cosies Up To Donald Trump, Lisa Nandy Could Be About To Become The Most Consequential Culture Secretary In America In Recent Memory Apple TV Seeking Local Language Boss Catherine Zeta-Jones Thriller ‘Kill Jackie’ Sells To AMC+ View All July 22, 2026 1:58am Hugh Laurie in 'Tehran' Apple TV EXCLUSIVE: Apple TV is seeking a head of local language scripted projects. The move comes amid a shuffling of the pack after Morgan Wandell and Oliver Jones exited the streamer, with Europe chief Jay Hunt handed a wider remit. Apple confirmed the Head of Local Language Scripted is a newly-created London-based role in Hunt’s team. According to a job ad posted on LinkedIn, the Head of Local Language Scripted will “work closely with experts in market” and the Creative Director of International TV [Hunt] to set strategy and commission content.” Related Stories Casting Corey Stoll, Ralph Ineson & Nyasha Hatendi Join Wyatt Russell-Led Monsterverse Series Casting Lewis Pullman Joins Julia Garner In 'Guilty Creatures' Apple TV Series From Tomorrow Studios The person is tasked with “defining and executing a comprehensive content strategy for local language scripted series across key international markets” and making “a compelling slate of shows that resonate in multiple markets,” according to the job ad. They will assess scripts for “Apple series potential” and meet with top talent and agents. Watch on Deadline Notably, the ad also says the person will need to “navigate regional nuance,” “leveraging a deep understanding of international market dynamics, including regional content quotas and local compliance, to identify whitespace opportunities in highly competitive territories.” The role is an opportunity for someone to set the direction of a big-budget streamer’s international slate and will likely attract a high caliber of candidate. Apple TV doesn’t make a huge amount of content outside of the States but under Hunt’s leadership it has delivered some big hits. Hunt’s UK slate includes the likes of Slow Horses, Bad Sisters and Hijack. Outside the UK it has made French fare like the Eva Green-starring Liaison and Gaumont’s The Hunt, Spain’s Women in Blue and Germany’s Where’s Wanda?, the latter two of which soon launch second seasons. Apple also picked up Legendary’s French-Japanese series Drops of God and is a co-producer on big hits like Tehran. The hire will come following exits in the Apple international team. International development chief Wandell left to launch his own indie while international scripted executive Jones moved to Amazon . The highly-rated Hunt’s remit has expanded to all international and local-language original projects. title: BBC Considers Locking Down iPlayer For Viewers Who Refuse To Pay Licence Fee
full_text: Skip to main content By Jake Kanter Jake Kanter International Investigations Editor @Jake_Kanter More Stories By Jake BBC Considers Locking Down iPlayer For Viewers Who Refuse To Pay Licence Fee Rebel Wilson Triumphs In Explosive Court Battle With ‘The Deb’ Co-Star Charlotte MacInnes BBC Staff In Town Hall Revolt Over 1% Pay Rise, Job Cuts & Romesh Ranganathan Ad: “It’s Insulting” View All July 22, 2026 1:36am 'The Traitors' host Claudia Winkleman BBC/Studio Lambert EXCLUSIVE : The BBC has said it is open-minded about closing off iPlayer to British viewers who decline to pay the licence fee. In an all-staff call on Monday, the BBC’s chief customer officer Kerris Bright said locking down iPlayer could be an “opportunity” in the future as the corporation looks to stem the tide of people opting out of the licence fee. Bright told colleagues that blocking iPlayer does not currently make sense because it could cause unwelcome friction for viewers who do pay the licence fee, but she said the BBC will keep the idea under review as streaming technology improves. Related Stories News BBC Staff In Town Hall Revolt Over 1% Pay Rise, Job Cuts & Romesh Ranganathan Ad: "It's Insulting" News Gordon Ramsay Profiled In BBC Landmark Series 'Rise Of The Rockstar Chef' “iPlayer is the only service really that’s licensable where you could actually decide to put a gate in front of it, but the data today says that actually [there’s] not really the return on investment for doing that,” Bright told employees, per audio obtained by Deadline. Watch on Deadline “The vast majority of evasion is not on iPlayer. The vast majority of people that are watching iPlayer are people that are paying their TV licence, and so to put a gate in front of iPlayer, the data today says that you actually would make it a very difficult experience for people that are paying for the licence.” iPlayer viewers do have to sign into the BBC to watch the streamer. The service also occasionally asks users if they have a licence fee, though it does not require proof. “It’s quite a difficult experience if you have to ask them to find their TV licence to put it into iPlayer,” Bright said. She continued: “As we go into the future, more of an IPTV world, audiences changing behavior, new ways of creating experiences that maybe aren’t so full of friction for audiences … that might absolutely be an opportunity for us, depending on the funding model. “The data says today [that] absolutely [it] is not the silver bullet to generating more revenue. There’s probably more cost in it than revenue, but it is something we should absolutely keep looking at because it might be something that you actually would want to do in the future.” More than half a million people opted out of the licence fee in the BBC’s most recent financial year, contributing to a funding crisis for the British broadcaster. Some 94% of the UK population use the BBC every month, but fewer than 80% pay the £180 annual licence fee. The BBC is attempting to bridge this gap and can no longer dip into its cash reserves to fund operations. In comments earlier this month, BBC director general Matt Brittin said: “We can and should be doing as much as possible on [enforcing] licence fee collection under the current model, but it’s yesterday’s model, it’s a busted flush, it’s no longer fit for purpose, and that’s why we need to make the case for the BBC and a shift of the licence fee model.” The BBC is in talks with the government about a new charter, which will likely lead to funding model changes. The BBC is not being prescriptive about what could replace the licence fee, but thinks the government should reconsider a compulsory household levy, which it previously ruled out. Another idea under consideration is extending the licence fee to include subscribers to streamers like Netflix, Disney+ and Prime Video. The plan has already drawn the ire of the Motion Picture Association, representing U.S. streamers and studios. title: Rebel Wilson Cleared in ‘The Deb’ Defamation Case
full_text: × Skip to main content By Abid Rahman Plus Icon Abid Rahman International Editor, Digital gentlemanabroad Follow Sign Up View All July 22, 2026 1:29am Rebel Wilson and Charlotte MacInnes. Sitthixay Ditthavong / Sydney Morning Herald via Getty Images; Don Arnold/Getty Images A Sydney court has cleared Rebel Wilson of defamation claims made by Australian actress Charlotte MacInnes, who starred in Wilson’s 2024 film The Deb . MacInnes had sued Wilson for Instagram posts the Pitch Perfect star had made on her own account in 2024 and 2025 that saw Wilson accuse MacInnes of retracting her complaint of sexual harassment against a film producer so she could further her career. MacInnes has denied Wilson’s claim. On Wednesday, the BBC reports that Australian federal court jude Elizabeth Raper dismissed the case against Wilson, saying that the claim that MacInnes had changed her story about the incident was not defamatory and that the actress had failed to establish the posts had caused or were likely to serious harm. Additionally, MacInnes was ordered to pay Wilson’s legal costs. Related Stories Movies 'The Brutalist' EP, 'Nawi' Makers Strike Three-Film Co-Pro Deal, Set Kenya Soccer Doc 'Champions of Nowhere' (Exclusive) Movies Nicolas Winding Refn Reveals the Chaotic Making of 'Drive': "Most Everyone Hated It" The defamation sprung from a September 2023 incident, where The Deb’s lead actress MacInnes went for a swim at Bondi Beach with Amanda Ghost , a British entertainment executive, a former president of Epic Records and one of the producers of Wilson’s film. According to the BBC, Ghost suffered an allergic reaction and both Ghost and MacInnes returned to the producer’s apartment, where they ended up in the bath together wearing swimsuits. Justice Raper cited the bath incident in her decision. “To say that the circumstance surrounding how they came to be in the bath are unusual would be an understatement,” she told the court. The Sydney defamation case is part of a larger set of legal cases related to The Deb , the Australian musical film comedy that Wilson produced, directed and had a support role in. In July 2024, Wilson revealed the acrimony behind the scenes, accusing the film’s producers Gregor Cameron, Vince Holden and Ghost of blocking the film’s premiere at the Toronto International Film Festival as well as making claims of embezzlement of $900,000 from the The Deb ‘s budget and Ghost’s inappropriate behavior towards the film’s lead actress MacInnes. In response, Cameron, Holden and Ghost filed a defamation lawsuit against Wilson. In January , a Los Angeles judge has tossed most of Wilson‘s lawsuit against The Deb producers, calling many of the allegations “conclusory without specific facts” and “abusive.” THR

title: Elon Musk Claims Grok Imagine Will Create “Historically Accurate” AI Version Of ‘The Odyssey’

The tech mogul wrote on his social media service X that his AI tool Grok Imagine would create a “full-length” feature version of Homer’s Greek myth. “Before this year ends, Grok Imagine will make a full-length movie of The Odyssey that is historically accurate and true to the art of Homer,” he wrote, sharing an AI-generated short of the story posted by an X user. Before this year ends, Grok Imagine will make a full-length movie of The Odyssey that is historically accurate and true to the art of Homer https://t.co/bVHzUmY9WN — Elon Musk (@elonmusk) July 22, 2026 Given that Homer’s story is a work of fiction, its questionable how “historically accurate” any remake can be, but X and Tesla owner Musk has repeatedly questioned the casting of Nolan’s picture over recent months. I’m down — Elon Musk (@elonmusk) July 22, 2026 Despite Musk’s opprobrium, two-time Oscar winner Nolan’s Odyssey has been a huge box office hit globally and looks set to become the British director’s highest-grossing film. full_text: Jul 22, 2026 1:15am PT Han Chae Young Scores a Hit With FlareFlow Korean Remake of Global Microdrama ‘Raising His Mistress’s Child’ By Naman Ramachandran Plus Icon Naman Ramachandran Latest Tak Sakaguchi Joins Iko Uwais in ‘Pendekar: Warrior’ as Nemesis (EXCLUSIVE) 13 minutes ago Rebel Wilson Wins Defamation Case Against Charlotte MacInnes Over ‘The Deb’ Bath Dispute 4 hours ago FAST Provider Wedotv Launches in India With Samsung TV Plus (EXCLUSIVE) 1 day ago See All FlareFlow Han Chae Young , the actor behind some of K-drama’s most recognized titles, has scored a hit with “Raising His Mistress’s Child,” a Korean-language remake of a global microdrama that generated more than $15 million in revenue. The series premiered July 10 on mobile-first platform FlareFlow and has already cleared 10 million cumulative views. The original production of “Raising His Mistress’s Child” drew some 4.8 million viewers and logged more than 513,700 completed viewers – figures that prompted COL Group’s international platform to commission a localized version aimed at Korean audiences. Related Stories ESPN Hit With Layoffs After Combining NFL Network Operations ESPN Plunges Back Into Shohei Ohtani Money Transfer Scandal With New Tisha Thompson Podcast The remake follows a woman whose survival of a difficult childbirth is compounded by the discovery that the baby placed in her arms belongs to her husband’s mistress, while her own newborn has been taken away. She sets out to reclaim her child and hold those responsible to account. Popular on Variety Early engagement metrics have been notable. The Korean adaptation recorded an 84.4% first-episode retention rate and a 67.6% second-episode retention rate. Han Chae Young is best known for her roles in “Boys Over Flowers” and “Autumn in My Heart.” Her casting follows a wider shift across the region, with established film and television talent moving into mobile-first productions as the microdrama format draws larger audiences and higher production investment. Timothy Oh, general manager of COL Group International and chief marketing officer at FlareFlow, said: “Microdrama has evolved far beyond short-form entertainment. Today, audiences expect premium storytelling, cinematic production quality and performances from actors they know and love. Han Chae Young’s decision to headline the Korean version of ‘Raising His Mistress’s Child’ reflects how far the industry has come. Mainstream television stars increasingly recognize microdramas as a premium storytelling format capable of reaching millions of deeply engaged viewers. This marks the beginning of a new era, where the biggest stories, the best talent, and the most compelling entertainment experiences are increasingly being created for the vertical screen.” FlareFlow, which has released approximately 5,200 series on its platform, operates in 14 languages and counts 33 million registered users across more than 200 countries and regions. title: Rebel Wilson Wins Defamation Case Against Charlotte MacInnes Over ‘The Deb’ Bath Dispute
full_text: Jul 22, 2026 12:16am PT Rebel Wilson Wins Defamation Case Against Charlotte MacInnes Over ‘The Deb’ Bath Dispute By Naman Ramachandran Plus Icon Naman Ramachandran Latest Tak Sakaguchi Joins Iko Uwais in ‘Pendekar: Warrior’ as Nemesis (EXCLUSIVE) 13 minutes ago Rebel Wilson Wins Defamation Case Against Charlotte MacInnes Over ‘The Deb’ Bath Dispute 4 hours ago FAST Provider Wedotv Launches in India With Samsung TV Plus (EXCLUSIVE) 1 day ago See All Variety via Getty Images Rebel Wilson has prevailed in a defamation case brought by Charlotte MacInnes , the lead actor in Wilson’s feature directorial debut, “ The Deb .” Justice Elizabeth Raper of the Federal Court of Australia in Sydney dismissed MacInnes’ defamation and breach-of-confidence claims on Wednesday and ordered her to pay Wilson’s legal costs. MacInnes sued over four sets of Instagram posts published between September 2024 and July 2025. She argued that the posts portrayed her as having changed her account of an incident involving “The Deb” producer Amanda Ghost in order to benefit her career. Related Stories ESPY Awards 2026 Livestream: Here's How to Watch the Sports Awards Ceremony Online John Lasker, a Top ESPN Streaming Executive, to Exit in September The dispute centred on events on Sept. 5, 2023, when MacInnes and Ghost went swimming at Bondi Beach before returning to the Sydney apartment where they were staying. Popular on Variety Ghost said she suffered a severe reaction to the cold water. MacInnes and Ghost subsequently entered a bath together while still wearing their swimsuits. Wilson alleged that MacInnes told her the following day that the situation had made her uncomfortable and later changed her story. MacInnes denied making such a complaint and said she had not felt uncomfortable. “To say that the circumstances surrounding how they came to be in the bath are unusual would be an understatement,” Raper wrote. The judge found that only one of the meanings alleged by MacInnes was conveyed by any of the four posts. That meaning, contained in Wilson’s Sept. 23, 2024, post, was that MacInnes had changed her account of feeling uncomfortable about what happened in the bathroom. Raper ruled that the meaning was not defamatory, finding that an assertion that someone had changed their story would not necessarily cause an ordinary reasonable person to think less of them. The judge also made two alternative findings. She ruled that MacInnes had not shown that the post caused, or was likely to cause, serious harm to her reputation. She further found that Wilson had established that the conveyed meaning was substantially true. Raper found on the balance of probabilities that MacInnes had felt uncomfortable about the situation, had said something about it to Wilson the following day and later changed her account. The judge described the versions given by MacInnes and Ghost as inconsistent and called both women “unreliable historians.” Raper found that Ghost, then 49, had placed MacInnes, then 25, in an awkward and stressful position by requiring the younger actor to care for her during the episode. The judgment did not determine that Ghost’s behaviour was sexual or that sexual harassment had occurred. Raper explicitly said the parties had not asked the court to decide whether the conduct had a sexualized dimension. The court instead found that MacInnes had complained about feeling uncomfortable in a situation that could be explained without reference to sexual harassment. MacInnes had argued that Wilson’s posts exposed her to online abuse and harmed her professional standing. Raper found no evidence that the posts had deprived her of work or other career opportunities. The judgment noted that MacInnes signed a recording contract with Atlantic Records in October 2025 and subsequently released two singles. Her agent did not identify any specific opportunity lost because of Wilson’s publications. MacInnes’ breach-of-confidence claim also failed. Raper found that MacInnes had not established that she made a complaint of sexual harassment, which was the information she alleged Wilson had improperly disclosed. The judge also found that the other required elements of the claim had not been proven. During closing arguments in May, MacInnes’ barrister, Sue Chrysanthou SC, strongly attacked Wilson’s credibility. “She is a fantastical liar who has made up terrible, terrible allegations about multiple people and her own witnesses have discredited her,” Chrysanthou told the court. Raper said in the judgment that she had serious concerns about the reliability of the evidence given by MacInnes, Ghost and Wilson. She nevertheless accepted Wilson’s evidence on the central question of whether MacInnes had raised her discomfort and subsequently changed her position. The ruling concerns MacInnes’ Australian case against Wilson. Separate legal disputes involving Wilson and producers of “The Deb” remain distinct from the proceeding. Following the decision, Wilson thanked the court for considering the evidence and said she was relieved that the proceedings had concluded. “This has been a long process and throughout I have tried to stay grounded in my values and what I believed was right,” Wilson said in a statement. Wilson also reiterated her support for “The Deb,” describing it as a film about individuality, resilience and the courage to be oneself. “Right now, what I’m focused on is what matters most, my family and my work,” she said. Wilson also addressed the ruling in an Instagram post , writing that she was “grateful this chapter has reached its conclusion” and was looking forward to returning to “what I love most, my family and my work.” She thanked “the Judge and the Australian legal system,” saying she has “the utmost respect” for both. ‘S.W.A.T’ Spinoff ‘Exiles’ Lands at Starz, Sets September Release Date Longtime Disney Exec Jim Kapenstein, Who Helped Oversee Some of Its Biggest Acquisitions, Joins Starz as Chief Legal and Strategy Officer ‘Power: Legacy’ Starring Joseph Sikora, Michael Rainey Jr. Greenlit at Starz Starz Acquires Russell T Davies’s Drama ‘Tip Toe’ for the U.S. and Canada Alliance of Women Directors and Starz Reveal Creative Leadership Lab’s Inaugural Class Designed to Support Female TV Directors (EXCLUSIVE) Starz to Develop ‘Bone Parish’ Series Executive Produced by Curtis ’50 Cent’ Jackson Loading comments...
