title: Rebel Wilson Triumphs In Explosive Court Battle With ‘The Deb’ Co-Star Charlotte MacInnes
full_text: Skip to main content By Jake Kanter Jake Kanter International Investigations Editor @Jake_Kanter More Stories By Jake BBC Considers Locking Down iPlayer For Viewers Who Refuse To Pay Licence Fee Rebel Wilson Triumphs In Explosive Court Battle With ‘The Deb’ Co-Star Charlotte MacInnes BBC Staff In Town Hall Revolt Over 1% Pay Rise, Job Cuts & Romesh Ranganathan Ad: “It’s Insulting” View All July 21, 2026 10:45pm Charlotte MacInnes and Rebel Wilson Getty Rebel Wilson did not defame Charlotte MacInnes , the Australian actress who stars in her directorial debut The Deb , a Sydney court has ruled. After an explosive and often bitter court battle earlier this year, Wilson has emerged victorious, saying she was “thankful” that the dispute had concluded for now. MacInnes plans to appeal, Deadline understands. There are also numerous other legal fronts in the messy wrangle over The Deb , including unresolved arguments between Wilson and the producers. MacInnes claimed that Wilson damaged her reputation by alleging that she was sexually harassed by The Deb producer, Amanda Ghost , and then colluded with Ghost to cover up what took place. Related Stories Trailers Rebel Wilson's 'The Deb' Gets First Trailer & Digital Release Date; Pic Sparked High-Profile Legal Battles Legal Rebel Wilson Branded A "Fantastical Liar" After Claiming 'The Deb' Actress Made Harassment Complaint At the heart of the case is an incident in September 2023, when Ghost shared a bath with MacInnes in a Sydney apartment after they had been swimming on Bondi beach. Ghost and MacInnes do not deny that they bathed together , but both say it was a non-sexual encounter, during which they were wearing swimsuits, after Ghost experienced a medical incident. Watch on Deadline Wilson claimed that MacInnes told her that the incident made the young actress uncomfortable. Wilson further alleged that MacInnes later withdrew her complaint after being given career opportunities by Ghost. MacInnes denied that the incident made her uncomfortable, suing Wilson over four Instagram posts, in which the Bridesmaids star discussed the events. Justice Elizabeth Raper, the Australian judge who presided over the case, agreed with Wilson. “MacInnes changed her story about feeling uncomfortable about what happened in the bathroom,” the judge ruled, adding that Wilson’s social media posts about MacInnes were not defamatory “by reason of these findings.” Delivering her verdict on Wednesday in Sydney, the judge added: “MacInnes has failed to establish that the publications have caused or were likely to cause serious harm.” In a statement, Wilson said: “I’m grateful the court has taken the time to carefully consider the evidence and reach its decision. This has been a long process and throughout I have tried to stay grounded in my values and what I believed was right. I’m thankful that process has now reached its conclusion. “I have always believed in the importance of telling stories that reflect real experiences and give people the opportunity to see themselves represented. The Deb is a story I’m incredibly passionate about because it celebrates individuality, resilience and the courage to be yourself. I hope audiences will experience the story for themselves and connect with the values at its heart. “Right now, what I’m focused on is what matters most, my family and my work.” MacInnes has been approached for comment. The ruling will not only be a blow to the actress, but also to Ghost and other The Deb producers, who gave evidence in support of MacInnes. They are suing Wilson in the LA Superior Court, accusing the Pitch Perfect star of pursuing an unjustified vendetta after being denied a writing credit on The Deb . Justice Raper was not convinced by the testimony of MacInnes, Ghost, and Wilson, but ultimately deemed that Wilson’s actions after the bathing incident supported her story. “I have serious misgivings as to the truthfulness of the testimony of each of Ms MacInnes, Ms Ghost and Ms Wilson,” the judge said. “Whilst I accept that Ms Wilson has a propensity to exaggerate events and her evidence could be found to be untruthful on occasion, the evidence establishes that Ms Wilson acted in a manner consistent with a complaint being made and her own contemporaneous documentary trail supports this.” The ruling comes as Sunrise Films has set an August 25 U.S. digital release date for The Deb . Wilson’s second feature, Girl Group , will also premiere at the Toronto International Film Festival. title: Sony LIV Welcomes Graphic India’s Animated Series ‘The Legend Of Karna’
full_text: Skip to main content By Jesse Whittock Jesse Whittock International TV Co-Editor @WhittockTweets More Stories By Jesse Elon Musk Claims Grok Imagine Will Create “Historically Accurate” AI Version Of ‘The Odyssey’ PBS Dials Up Deal For Alternative Music Doc ‘CFNY: The Spirit Of Radio’ Plimsoll Unveils Studios Rebrand & Rejigs With U.S. Chief Alan Eyres Overseeing Creative Divisions View All July 21, 2026 10:30pm 'The Legend Of Karna' Sony LIV EXCLUSIVE: Sony LIV is getting deeper into the animation game. The India-based streamer has quietly greenlit The Legend of Karna , which comes from Sharad Devarajan and Jeevan J. Kang and their production house, Graphic India . The series, inspired by the Hindu epic Mahabharata, will debut on July 31. The Legend of Karna will follow Karna, a warrior from the Mahabharata who is born to Kunti and the Sun God Surya but raised in a charioteer’s household. He possesses the gift of bow mastery that rivals the greatest warriors of his age, compelling him to enter a grand tournament usually reserved only for kings and princes, altering the course of an empire forever. Related Stories Acquisitions Sony LIV Lands Aamir Khan's Feel-Good Basketball Comedy-Drama 'Sitaare Zameen Par' News 'Real Kashmir Football Club' Adds The Drama To A 'Welcome To Wrexham'-Like Story From India - Global Breakouts Per the synopsis: “Crowned king by the ambitious prince Duryodhana but rejected by a court that judges him by his perceived birth, Karna rises from the dust of a village workshop to the royal halls of Hastinapura. His journey is one of exceptional talent, unwavering loyalty and an enduring struggle against a world governed by lineage. As politics, prophecy and hidden truths converge, Karna must confront the conflict between the destiny imposed upon him and the legacy he seeks to create.” Watch on Deadline “We couldn’t imagine a better home for this journey than with our partners at Sony LIV and the incredible honor they are giving us,” said Devarajan, the co-creator and showrunner. “Karna has always been the tragic heart of the Mahabharata. What the world refused him by birth, his own hands took with skill, and that question feels more urgent today than ever. Does greatness come from where you are born, or from what you do? “With The Legend of Karna, we wanted to tell his story the way it deserves to be told, as an epic saga of unbreakable loyalty, unbearable secrets, and a talent that shook the world, seen through the eyes of a hero who has always stood at the center of the epic but never at the center of its telling.” Devarajan is known for collaborating with the late Stan Lee, collaborating to create Indian superhero series Chakra – The Invincible for Cartoon Network India in 2013. The Marvel creator’s influence on Devarajan was such that in the past he has been called “the Indian Stan Lee.” His credits include producing Netflix live-action film The Archies and The Legend of Hanuman , which has become one of the top-rated series on Disney+ Hotstar. Devarajan also created the Spider-Man India character for Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse , and teamed with SS Rajamouli on the Baahubali movie franchise, overseeing gaming, publishing and digital media and creating Prime Video series Baahubali: The Lost Legends . More recently, we revealed he is working with The Adjustment Bureau writer-director George Nolfi on a Hindi-language version of the 2011 film, and with Gladiator writer David Franzoni to turn his graphic novel about Alexander the Great into a scripted series alongside Starlings Television. As for the Sony Pictures Networks India-owned Sony LIV, which is known for originals such as Rocket Boys , Scam and Freedom at Midnight , the series joins other animated originals such as Jiyo Gopuda . title: Zendaya’s Best Press Tour Looks, From ‘Dune’ to ‘Challengers’ to ‘The Odyssey’
full_text: × Skip to main content By Carly Thomas Plus Icon Carly Thomas Senior Editor, Digital carly_thomas_ Follow Sign Up View All July 21, 2026 9:42pm Published on July 21, 2026 Zendaya for 'Spider-Man: No Way Home,' Dune: Part Two,' 'Challengers' and 'The Drama' press tours. Amy Sussman/Getty Images; Jeff Spicer/Getty Images for Warner Bros. Pictures; Gareth Cattermole/Getty Images; Dia Dipasupil/Getty Images One thing about Zendaya: She and her longtime stylist, Law Roach, are always going to deliver on a red carpet. The pair has become known for fully committing to a theme, specifically when it comes to dressing for a film or television show’s press tour. They’ve become modern pioneers of “method dressing,” where they match Zendaya’s wardrobes on the red carpet to her projects’ themes and narratives. From the Dune films to Challengers to the Spider-Man movies and The Odyssey , the actress has turned promotional opportunities into high-fashion moments with archival looks, bold outfits and designs that align with her characters. Below, The Hollywood Reporter ranks Zendaya’s press tour styling over the years. ‘Spider-Man: Homecoming’ Image Credit: Alberto E. Rodriguez/Getty Images; Carlos Alvarez/Getty Images As Zendaya started pivoting away from Disney Channel, she scored her first Hollywood blockbuster role in 2017’s Spider-Man: Homecoming . It’s also where she met her now-husband, Tom Holland, who plays Peter Parker. With this being her first major press tour, Zendaya still seemed to be finding her voice in fashion. Though her looks weren’t fully cohesive and aligned with a theme just yet, she and Roach still brought stunning looks to the red carpet. At the film’s L.A. premiere, Zendaya wore a pink Ralph & Russo gown, Casadei heels and Bulgari jewels. At a photocall in Madrid, Spain, she sported a blue Jonathan Simkhai dress and Rihanna x Manolo Blahnik heels. See more of her Spider-Man: Homecoming press tour looks here . ‘Space Jam: A New Legacy’ Image Credit: Kevin Winter/Getty Images For the Los Angeles premiere of 2021’s Space Jam: A New Legacy , Zendaya knew she had one moment to make a statement and she left it all on the table (or the red carpet, so to speak). The actress didn’t do a ton of press for this live-action/animated hybrid film, so she really made the premiere a moment with a colorful look, channeling Lola Bunny, the character she voiced. Zendaya wore a colorblock ensemble that featured a jacket and short shorts by Moschino. She paired it with Bulgari small hoop earrings and rings, as well as white stiletto heels. ‘Euphoria’ Image Credit: Monica Schipper/Getty Images; Jeff Kravitz/FilmMagic for HBO; Frazer Harrison/Getty Images The premieres for Euphoria — the hit HBO show that earned Zendaya her first two Emmys and cemented her status as a serious dramatic actress post-Disney — really highlight her style evolution over the years. While there’s not a set theme for her looks, as there weren’t lengthy global press tours for each of the show’s seasons, she still managed to serve on the carpet. For season one in 2019, Zendaya went for a more innocent look with a white, spaghetti-strap mini dress, featuring a sheer ruffle detail, from the Nina Ricci Fall 2019 collection. She paired it with white Le Silla heels. Fast forward to 2022, and Zendaya delivered an edgier look at the season two premiere, similar to the show’s second installment. She wore a vintage Valentino black-and-white striped form-fitting dress. And more recently, at the season three premiere in 2026, the actress stepped out in a much more mature look from Ashi Studio. The deep brown sleeveless halter dress featured a high neckline, low back and flowy train. ‘Spider-Man: Far From Home’ Image Credit: Kevin Winter/Getty Images; Jeff Spicer/Getty Images for Sony For the Spider-Man: Far from Home press tour in 2019, Zendaya brought her own version of the famed superhero to the red carpet. Though she plays MJ, Peter Parker’s romantic interest in the film franchise, the actress channeled Spider-Man himself at the Los Angeles premiere, sporting a geometric red and black sequin Armani gown. She paired the dress with black Christian Louboutin “So Kate” pumps (which have become her signature red carpet shoe). “If Spider-Man were a girl, this would be her Spidey suit or her uniform,” Roach previously told THR of the look. As for the London photo call at the Tower of London, Zendaya stood on business in a chic, structured Alexandre Vauthier suit, paired with a white button-down shirt and black Christian Louboutin pumps. She also tied the look all together with red hair, a nod to Peter Parker’s favorite color. ‘The Greatest Showman’ Image Credit: Lisa Maree Williams/Getty Images; Carlos Tischler/SOPA Images/LightRocket via Getty Images; Michael Loccisano/Getty Images The press tour for 2017’s The Greatest Showman was really the first time Zendaya fully leaned into cohesive themed looks for the red carpet. She and Roach went the whimsical route, inspired by the movie’s circus and ethereal creatures. Some of the most memorable outfits include a custom Moschino monarch butterfly dress designed by Jeremy Scott, paired with black strappy heels, for the Australian premiere. At the film’s premiere in Mexico City, the actress sported a ringmaster-inspired look from Ralph Lauren Collection. It featured a red tuxedo jacket, black tuxedo trousers and a white collared shirt, paired with black pumps. Lastly, but certainly not least, Zendaya stunned at the world premiere in New York City in a black and red tiered tulle ball gown from the Viktor & Rolf Spring 2018 collection. ‘Spider-Man: No Way Home’ Image Credit: Amy Sussman/Getty Images; Gareth Cattermole/Getty Images Zendaya was really channeling her Spidey-senses for the Spider-Man: No Way Home press tour in 2021. The Emmy winner stepped in multiple looks that featured spiderweb motifs — it doesn’t get much more on brand than that. At the world premiere in Los Angeles, she left everyone speechless (even Holland, as seen in an interview with Entertainment Tonight ) once she arrived, wearing a sheer, beige custom Valentino gown covered in black spiderweb embroidery. She also paired it with a spiderweb-designed lace mask and Christian Louboutin pumps. Zendaya brought more spiderwebs to the London photo call with her menswear-inspired Alexander McQueen outfit. The look included an oversized gray blazer embellished with a crystal spiderweb-like design and thigh-high black boots with the same embellishments. She also wore spiderweb earrings. ‘Dune: Part One’ Image Credit: Vittorio Zunino Celotto/Getty Images; Jeff Spicer/Jeff Spicer/Getty Images for Warner BrosKristy Sparow/Getty Images; If Zendaya’s Dune: Part One red carpet outfits had your jaw on the floor (truthfully, mine was), just wait until we get to Part Two . But first, let’s take a moment to appreciate the high-fashion looks that kicked off the film franchise’s promotions. The actress and Roach were fully committed to the movie’s desert and warrior themes with her attire during the 2021 press run. At the Venice Film Festival premiere, she walked the carpet in a custom, wet-look Balmain gown. The dress’s sand-colored leather was molded to her body for a sculpted, draped effect. When Zendaya attended the Paris photocall at Le Grand Rex, she wore a plum two-piece Maison Alaïa look that featured a knitted, backless top and a matching pencil skirt with an extravagant fur hem. At the Dune U.K. Special Screening in London, she arrived in a Rick Owens sculptural sequin cream-colored gown that felt quite sci-fi inspired. ‘Challengers’ Image Credit: James Gourley/Getty Images; Gareth Cattermole/Getty Images; Monica Schipper/Getty Images Zendaya is used to bringing the heat to the screen rather than the court, but that doesn’t mean she can’t shut down a red carpet with some Tennis-inspired looks when needed. And it was absolutely needed (and wanted by her fans) during the press tour for Luca Guadagnino’s Challengers . The actress started on a high note at the Australian premiere with a look that screamed tenniscore: a custom Loewe gown designed by Jonathan Anderson — who costumed the film — that featured a tennis player and ball motif. Zendaya continued the theme at the London premiere with a white sequined country club-esque halter dress by Thom Browne that also featured a tennis racket motif. As for the Los Angeles premiere, though the actress stepped away from tenniscore for a moment, she still served in a Vera Wang black-and-pink gown. Some other honorable mentions that also deserve to be highlighted below include the archival look by Vivienne Westwood from the Spring/Summer 1994 “Café Society” ready-to-wear collection, a custom silver-colored Loewe tennis dress and a retro-chic mini dress from the Louis Vuitton by Marc Jacobs archives. Zendaya for ‘Challengers’ press tour. Jeff Spicer/Getty Images; Vittorio Zunino Celotto/Getty Images; Kristy Sparow/Getty Images ‘The Drama’ Image Credit: Phillip Faraone/Getty Images; Aurore Marechal/Getty Images; Massimo Insabato/Archivio Massimo Insabato/Mondadori Portfolio via Getty Images; Dia Dipasupil/Getty Images Zendaya had a specific theme in mind going into The Drama press tour. With the dark romantic comedy/psychological thriller being centered on a wedding that goes wrong, the actress chose to stun in several bridal-inspired looks that align with the wedding tradition of “something old, something new, something borrowed, something blue.” The actress kicked things off with “something old” at the L.A. premiere, sporting a white Vivienne Westwood gown that she previously wore at the 2015 Academy Awards. Next came “something new,” and she wore a custom Louis Vuitton dress that featured an oversized black bow in the back at the Paris premiere. As for “something borrowed,” Cate Blanchett loaned Zendaya a black dress made custom by Giorgio Armani Privé for the film’s Rome premiere. Lastly, but certainly not least, she stunned at the New York premiere in a black and blue Schiaparelli gown to complete her method dressing streak with “something blue.” ‘Dune: Part Two’ Image Credit: Dimitrios Kambouris/Getty Images; Jeff Spicer/Getty Images for Warner Bros. Pictures; Angel Delgado/Getty Images; Gareth Cattermole/Getty Images It’s safe to say that some of Zendaya’s looks during the Dune: Part Two press tour will definitely be remembered for decades to come. At every event for the film, she kept everyone stunned, delivering futuristic sci-fi and desert-chic looks that fully highlighted the film’s mood and themes. At the New York premiere, she wore a cream-colored gown with gold detailing at the hem, designed by Stéphane Rolland couture. One of Zendaya’s most daring and iconic looks to date was at the London premiere, where she sported an archival 1995 Thierry Mugler chrome cyborg suit . The actress’ method dressing continued at the premiere in Mexico City, wearing a custom Bottega Veneta two-piece look that featured a cropped wool flannel turtleneck and matching high-slit skirt. Then at a photo call in London, she turned heads in a “pre-runway” ensemble from Roksanda that featured a three-piece dark plum suit. See more memorable looks from the Dune: Part Two press tour here . ‘The Odyssey’ Image Credit: Jamie McCarthy/Getty Images; Aurore Marechal/Getty Images; Jeff Spicer/Getty Images for Universal Pictures; Shane Anthony Sinclair/Getty Images Zendaya quite literally served goddess during the press tour for Christopher Nolan’s epic The Odyssey . The actress embodied her character in the film, Athena — the Greek goddess of wisdom and warfare who serves as Odysseus’s (Matt Damon) divine protector — on the many red carpets around the globe. In one of her first looks, she wore a custom ivory-colored Jacquemus draped dress with a low-plunging back for a photo call in London. She finished the look off with large Glenn Spiro gold earrings. Then at the Paris premiere, she stunned in a custom white-colored Louis Vuitton gown that features ruffle puff sleeves. At the world premiere held in London, Zendaya stunned in a couture Schiaparelli gown that was fresh off the runway earlier in the day. The dress featured a sculpted white bustier breastplate and a beaded fringe skirt. And the actress definitely saved one of her best looks for last with the jaw-dropping Matières Fécales dress at the New York City premiere. The look featured feather embellishments throughout, paired with large white feathered angel wings. *** Zendaya is booked and busy this year, with two more projects on the way: Spider-Man: Brand New Day and Dune: Part Three . And it’s safe to say I’m already sat at the dinner table, waiting for which stunning looks she’s going to feed everyone with next. THR

title: ‘Spider-Man: Brand New Day’ Final Trailer Shows Tom Holland Swinging With Zendaya & Closer Look At Mark Ruffalo’s Hulk

The sneak peek also shows Zendaya ‘s MJ swinging alongside Spider-Man and new fighting sequences between the web-slinger and Mark Ruffalo ‘s Hulk. Watch the trailer in the video above. Related Stories News Sadie Sink Keeps Mystery Of ‘Spider-Man: Brand New Day’ Role & Reveals “I Am Not Aunt May” News ‘Spider-Man: Brand New Day’ Red Carpet Photos: All Of Zendaya & Tom Holland’s Looks From Press Tour Destin Daniel Cretton directs Spider-Man: Brand New Day , which is set to open in theaters on July 31. The movie, based on the comic book by Stan Lee and Steve Ditko, was written by Chris McKenna and Erik Sommers. The logline for Spider-Man: Brand New Day reads, “It’s a brand new day for Peter Parker. title: Dixon Dern Dies: Attorney For CAA, CBS, TV Academy & Desilu, Was 97
full_text: Skip to main content By Tom Tapp Tom Tapp Deputy Managing Editor More Stories By Tom Dixon Dern Dies: Attorney For CAA, CBS, TV Academy & Desilu, Was 97 2026-27 Awards Season Calendar: Oscars, Emmys, Grammys, Guilds & More Jennifer Finch Dies: L7 Bassist Who Battled Brain Cancer Was 59 View All July 21, 2026 7:22pm Dixon Dern The Television Academy/YouTube Dixon Q. Dern, the longtime entertainment attorney who worked at CBS , Desilu and United Artists TV; who represented clients such as Desi Arnaz and the Television Academy; who often served as an arbitrator for labor disputes; who helped incorporate CAA and who was a pioneer in syndicated sales and pre-financing for TV, died on July 19. That, according to an obituary posted by Forest Lawn, Hollywood Hills. He was 97. Related Stories Obituaries 2026 Deaths Photo Gallery: Hollywood & Media Obituaries News Donald Trump Says ABC And NBC Should Lose Broadcast Licenses Because They Didn’t Carry His Primetime Address On Election Claims Dern began his career as an in-house counsel for United Artists Television, Desilu and CBS before founding his own firm where he represented Desi Arnaz, the Television Academy, Village Roadshow, the Bob Hope Estate and many others. Dern was often at the nexus of power in Hollywood. One day in the mid-seventies, Michael Ovitz showed up, closed Dern’s office door and told him he and his associates would be back for a meeting the next day. The subject of the proposed meeting was not disclosed. “The next day five guys showed up,” said Dern. “It was the five founders of CAA. They were basically the top five guys in William Morris’ television division and they told me they were leaving William Morris and wanted to set up a new agency. So I formed a corporation. They rented a little office with fold-up desks fold-up tables. We did their legal work for them for about six months.” Dern often served as an arbitrator under various industry collective bargaining agreements, specifically the DGA and WGA. Dern represented the Television Academy for decades. He played a key role in settling a long-simmering feud between its Los Angeles and New York contingents. From the Academy’s official history : By 1976, the situation had exacerbated to the point where, at the semiannual official national board meeting at the Beverly Wilshire Hotel, each chapter designated five-man negotiation teams to meet separately from the rest of the attendees for a two-day summit convened to broker a detente. The Los Angeles contingent consisted of Michael Baugh, Dixon Dern , Hank Rieger, the late Bob Sweeney and then Los Angeles chapter president Larry Stewart , who was chairman of the negotiating committee. Reaching a settlement took far longer than two days. Again, from the Academy’s official history: After much legal wrangling, the two groups agreed to sever in 1977. According to the terms of the dissolution, both would retain their national status, with the group in Los Angeles retaining its original name, the Academy of Television Arts & Sciences, and the New York body retaining the name of National Academy of Television Arts & Sciences. Each group would also control specific award shows: the Los Angeles-based Television Academy kept the Primetime Emmys, and jurisdiction over the Los Angeles chapter; the New York entity retained the Daytime, News, Documentary and Sports Emmys, as well as jurisdiction over the 19 chapters throughout the U.S. that produced their own local Emmy events. Dern also learned a lot about the business while in the legal department at CBS. “When I got there it was pre Fin-Syn, the financial interest syndication rule which later barred the networks from owning distribution rights,” he said in a 2021 interview with the Beverly Hills Bar Association, of which he was a former president. “But at the time…CBS could own its own shows. In fact, in most cases, it did and we did all the production work on them.” Among those shows were classics like Gunsmoke , Have Gun Will Travel and Perry Mason . Dern later moved to Desilu when Desi Arnaz and Lucille Ball were still involved there. “One thing we did there that was, I think, instrumental to the industry: We decided to set up a syndicated sales organization. We set up Desilu Sales which became the forerunner of Paramount Television today.” He left Desilu after Paramount acquired the company. When Dern set up his own practice, Desi became one of his first clients and stayed with him until the day the actor/producer died. Even after Desi’s death, Dern maintained a working relationship with the family. In fact, Dern represented Lucy and Desi Arnaz Jr. in negotiating rights for Amazon’s Being the Ricardos . Through the years, Dern also became involved in back-end television financing and pre-sales to foreign distributors. “I had a number of clients who were producing shows and they would get the funds from the network but they wouldn’t have enough money to to finish production,” he recalled. “So you’d go out and get distributors. They were guaranteed to put up the money on delivery. Then I would go to a bank — or really I went to Lew Horowitz. He would set up the financing with a bank and then as soon as the picture was delivered the distributor would pay the bank and everybody would be happy. It’s very similar to the structure that was done in the independent film market.” Services will be held at Forest Lawn, Hollywood Hills from 9:30 a.m. – 10 a.m. on July 31. title: Matt Damon Says His Female Stunt Double In ‘The Odyssey’ “Had The Greatest Arms I’ve Ever Seen”
full_text: Skip to main content By Armando Tinoco Armando Tinoco Staff Writer @armietinoco More Stories By Armando Matt Damon Says His Female Stunt Double In ‘The Odyssey’ “Had The Greatest Arms I’ve Ever Seen” Jacob Elordi On Filming ‘Euphoria’ Death Scene & Returning For Final Season: “I Think Sam Brought Me Back To Torture Me” ‘La La Land’s 10th Anniversary Poster Fixes Ryan Gosling’s Hand That Haunted Him View All July 21, 2026 7:15pm Matt Damon in 'The Odyssey' Melinda Sue Gordon / Universal Pictures Matt Damon physically transformed to play Odysseus in Christopher Nolan’s The Odyssey , but he also had some help from stunt performers in some key scenes. In a new interview, Damon revealed that one of his stunt doubles was a female standing in as the Greek hero as the character faces the giant Laestrygonians. “When we did the force perspective stuff with the Laestrygonians… there were these stuntmen who were all seven feet, and then they got stunt men who were under five feet,” Damon said on the Happy Sad Confused podcast. “My double was a woman, a female stunt performer who had the greatest arms I’ve ever seen.” Related Stories News ‘The Odyssey’s Tom Holland On Why He “Could Relax” After Robert Pattinson Casting: “You’re So Good At Being A Dickhead” News Christopher Nolan Says ‘The Odyssey’ Made Him “Hit The Limits Of My Own Stamina” & It’ll Be “At Least” 3 Years Until His Next Film He continued, “When she came into the catering tent, it was the first time I met her. I went up and hugged her and thanked her for all the work she put in.” Watch on Deadline Damon said that “there are those shots where you see these giants looming over me [and that’s her],” adding, “It was almost 100% my arms in the movie, but you got to give it up where it’s due.”

title: ‘In the City’ Reunion Recap: Lindsay and Whitney’s Heated Exchange, Amanda Reveals She and West Haven’t Said “I Love You”
full_text: Skip to main content By Lexi Carson Plus Icon Lexi Carson Associate Editor View All July 21, 2026 6:36pm 'In the City' reunion. Charles Sykes/Bravo Logo text [This story contains spoilers from part one of the In the City season one reunion.] There’s more drama in New York City. Bravo aired part one of the In the City reunion Tuesday night, bringing together Lindsay Hubbard , Kyle Cooke , Amanda Batula , Danielle Olivera, Eoin Heavey, Andrea Denver, Lexi Sundin, Nick Barber, Yvonne Najor, Georgina Ferzil, Whitney Fransway, Kenny Martin and Gavin Moseley for the Summer House spinoff’s first-ever reunion. Sitting down with Andy Cohen , the cast revisited the season’s biggest moments — and everything that’s unfolded since season one wrapped filming. Related Stories TV 'In the City' Concludes With Two-Part Reunion: Where to Watch 'Summer House' Spinoff's Cast Sit-Down Online TV Between 'Summer House' and 'In the City,' Lindsay Hubbard Doesn't Have Time to Slow Down The reunion opened with Amanda, who shared an update on her relationship with West Wilson. After addressing the viral photos from Italy that made it look like she was spitting into his mouth — “It was an olive,” she clarified — Amanda also reflected on the Summer House reunion , admitting she was on beta blockers too, not just West, and was “disappointed” by how defensive she came across. When Andy asked whether she and West had taken the next step and said “I love you,” Amanda initially responded, “Not really.” Pressed further, she confirmed they still haven’t — though the pair are now monogamous. (The reunion was filmed June 11.) Danielle admitted she was disappointed and shocked to learn Amanda and West were still together. “I honestly hate that you doubled down and are pursuing this relationship with him all the way, going to Italy with him,” Danielle said. “I know you had to make a decision one way or the other. I just wish, for you, that you would take some time just for yourself. Be alone.” Amanda and West publicly revealed they were dating just two months after she and Kyle announced the end of their marriage, drawing criticism from fans because West had previously been romantically involved with Amanda’s friend, Ciara Miller. Bravo also recently announced that Amanda won’t return for season 11 of Summer House , which is currently filming. Amanda went on to praise Kyle for supporting her through the backlash, despite their divorce. “I’m eternally grateful for Kyle. I think it just shows how much we do have love for each other, and I think it’s something that we’ve made clear from the very beginning,” she said. “Unfortunately, we could not communicate, we could not get along in our marriage, but that doesn’t take away from the friendship and the love that we’ve had for each other this whole time. Which is also why, when we first separated, I was standing up for him because I didn’t want people to attack him. And I’m just really appreciative that he’s been there for me as well because if anyone knows each other better, it’s the two of us.” Elsewhere, Kyle shared that he’s dating again and recently spent time with Southern Charm star Salley Carson, while Lindsay said she isn’t looking for anything serious. The night’s most heated exchange, however, centered on Lindsay and Whitney, whose feud over Whitney’s “single mom card” comment exploded during last week’s finale . During the finale, Whitney accused Lindsay of using the “single mom card” to get her way when she took the primary bedroom instead of giving it to Yvonne and Nick following the drama surrounding their wedding. Whitney also criticized Lindsay for having a nanny. “I almost threw up watching this,” Lindsay said. “It was so much worse than it was explained to me.” Despite Whitney’s repeated apologies, Lindsay wasn’t ready to forgive. “I cried about what you said for six months. You’re gonna need about 15 more apologies to any mom in the entire world,” Lindsay fired back. “Really poor choice of words.” Kenny defended Whitney, explaining earlier in the reunion that some of his actions throughout the season were influenced by grieving the loss of his mother. “Lindsay, you collect apologies like you collect souls,” he said. Gavin also clarified his earlier comments about Whitney, explaining that when he called her “boring” during the season, he was actually frustrated that she and Kenny were portraying their relationship as perfect while they were still working through issues behind the scenes. Some arguments remained unresolved, and based on the preview for part two, the tension is only escalating. Based on the teaser trailer, next week’s reunion sees Amanda once again accuse Kyle of having an affair during their marriage — but this time, she claims she only recently learned about it. The In the City reunion part two airs Tuesday, July 28, at 8 p.m. ET. All episodes of In the City are streaming on Peacock. THR

title: Games for Change 2026 Award Winners Revealed

title: Jacob Elordi On Filming ‘Euphoria’ Death Scene & Returning For Final Season: “I Think Sam Brought Me Back To Torture Me”
full_text: Skip to main content By Armando Tinoco Armando Tinoco Staff Writer @armietinoco More Stories By Armando Matt Damon Says His Female Stunt Double In ‘The Odyssey’ “Had The Greatest Arms I’ve Ever Seen” Jacob Elordi On Filming ‘Euphoria’ Death Scene & Returning For Final Season: “I Think Sam Brought Me Back To Torture Me” ‘La La Land’s 10th Anniversary Poster Fixes Ryan Gosling’s Hand That Haunted Him View All July 21, 2026 5:40pm Jacob Elordi Disney / Randy Holmes Jacob Elordi had a gruesome death on the final season of Euphoria and is giving insight into how that scene was shot. While making an appearance on ABC’s Jimmy Kimmel Live ! , Elordi had a mini Euphoria reunion with co-star Colman Domingo, who is guest-hosting the late-night talk show in Kimmel’s absence. Domingo brought up his character’s death in the HBO series with the audience audibly letting out an “Aww.” Related Stories News ‘Euphoria’ Season 3 Finale: How Things End For Zendaya’s Rue & The Rest Of The Cast News ‘Euphoria’ Season 3 Premiere Red Carpet Photos: Zendaya, Jacob Elordi, Sydney Sweeney, Hunter Schafer & More “Thanks,” Elordi jokingly said to the audience. “Finally, after eight years of all of you saying, ‘F*** Nate, kill Nate, Nate’s a piece of sh**…’ Yeah, I read your tweets… Now you’re sad.” Elordi played high school bully Nate, who in Season 3 dealt with some goons who tormented him and brutally physically assaulted him. Watch on Deadline “I think Sam [Levinson] brought me back to torture me,” the actor said of the series creator. “It was like, six weeks of me just getting beat by a seven-foot guy. I was just running the whole time.” For filming his character’s death, Elordi explained, “I got locked in a coffin for two days straight with prosthetics and a fake tongue, and I couldn’t see out of my eyes. And then Sam put snakes into the coffin.” Of his reptilian co-star, Elordi said, “he had a very nice personality,” adding, “He was very docile and very sleepy. I don’t think he’d had a lot of sun … So I was kind of nudging him, trying to get him to get active and do the scene, because he was meant to be threatening. But he was like a little dog. He just kept curling up at my feet.” Watch Elordi’s full interview in the video below. title: ‘La La Land’ 10th Anniversary Poster Finally Corrects Ryan Gosling’s Flat Hand
full_text: × Skip to main content By Lexi Carson Plus Icon Lexi Carson Associate Editor View All July 21, 2026 5:25pm 'La La Land' 2026 poster. Lionsgate Ryan Gosling can finally sleep at night. After nearly a decade of joking that his pose on the original La La Land poster “haunted” him, Lionsgate has finally fixed it with a new poster celebrating the film’s 10th anniversary. The updated artwork arrives as La La Land heads back to theaters beginning Aug. 16, when the Oscar-winning musical will return to Dolby Cinema at AMC Theatres for its anniversary re-release. A La La Land musical is also currently in development for Broadway . Related Stories Movies Nicolas Winding Refn Reveals the Chaotic Making of 'Drive': "Most Everyone Hated It" Movies 'Passion of the Christ' Set For Theatrical Return The original poster for the 2016 film features Emma Stone and Gosling dancing against the Los Angeles skyline. Both actors have one arm raised, but while Stone’s hand is extended up, Gosling tilted his hand flat — a choice he has since joked he regretted. Speaking to The Wall Street Journal in 2024, the Barbie star reflected on why the pose still “haunts” him. “Maybe La La Land ,” he said when asked if there was a role he’d like to do over. “There’s a moment that haunts me where we’re dancing, Emma and I, and I didn’t know this would become the poster for the movie, but I thought we were supposed to have our hands up and I thought it’d be cool to put my hand [flat].” “Even though everyone told me it wasn’t cool, I was sure it was cooler,” he continued. “Now when I look at it, and I have to see it all the time, you know what would’ve been cooler… [the correct pose]. It just killed the energy that way. I call it La La hand.” Gosling even poked fun at the moment earlier this year while promoting Project Hail Mary , releasing a video in March in which he finally corrected his “La La hand.” Directed by Damien Chazelle , La La Land follows aspiring actress Mia (Stone) and jazz musician Sebastian (Gosling) as they chase their Hollywood dreams while falling in love. The film won six Academy Awards, including best director for Chazelle and best actress for Stone. It is also remembered for the infamous best picture mix-up at the 2017 Oscars, when La La Land was mistakenly announced as the winner before it was revealed that Moonlight had actually taken home the award. La La Land poster 2026 Lionsgate THR

title: How Christopher Nolan’s ‘The Odyssey’ Conquered the Box Office
full_text: × Skip to main content By Ryan Gajewski Plus Icon Ryan Gajewski Senior Entertainment Reporter View All July 21, 2026 5:21pm Matt Damon and Zendaya in 'The Odyssey.' Universal Pictures/Courtesy Everett Collection Logo text Christopher Nolan ‘s box office journey with The Odyssey has gotten off to an eye-opening start . If there were any questions regarding whether a nearly three-hour, R-rated adaptation of a 2,700-year-old Greek poem — not to mention a movie that initially got caught up in the culture wars — would find success, those concerns were swiftly vanquished. In landing 2026’s biggest live-action opening weekend, The Odyssey brought home $123.5 million domestically with a global launch of $263.7 million, with the latter marking the accomplished filmmaker’s highest-grossing worldwide opening ever. Related Stories Movies Snoop Dogg Biopic Lands Summer 2027 Release From Universal Movies 'Other Mommy' Trailer: Jessica Chastain Returns to Horror as a Body-Twisting Doppelganger It’s not news that Nolan, hot off his first best director Oscar for 2023’s Oppenheimer , has become a brand unto himself as he continues his run as one of Hollywood’s most sure-thing filmmakers. But the fact that half of the movie’s audiences were between the ages of 18 and 34 confirmed that he has captured the excitement of Gen Z and younger millennial filmgoers as much as that of older viewers who might typically be more drawn to an adaptation of classic literature. Audiences cited Nolan as their top reason for seeing The Odyssey , according to PostTrak surveys, which is no small feat, given that the star-studded cast includes Matt Damon , Tom Holland , Robert Pattinson , Anne Hathaway , Lupita Nyong’o, Zendaya , Charlize Theron and no shortage of others. The Odyssey Universal “We had high expectations for the movie and high expectations for our performance, and it exceeded both,” IMAX CEO Richard Gelfond tells The Hollywood Reporter . Indeed, fans were focused on experiencing the film on IMAX after The Odyssey became the first narrative feature shot entirely on the company’s cameras. “Chris knows how to use IMAX technology and the IMAX brand as well as anyone on the planet.” The Odyssey is unlikely to drift away anytime soon, with Gelfond pointing out that IMAX presales for the Universal Pictures film’s forthcoming sophomore frame “are equivalent to some of our really best movies’ opening weekends.” Competing studios presumably anticipated the high demand, as there are no wide releases scheduled for this coming weekend in between The Odyssey and the July 31 release of Sony’s similarly anticipated Spider-Man: Brand New Day , representing a rarity for a coveted July frame. “Presales are just incredibly backloaded because we see people waiting to get the best seat that they can and see it in the way that the filmmaker intends it to be seen,” notes Fandango director of analytics and Box Office Theory founder Shawn Robbins. “There are plenty of theaters around the country where shows are almost — if not totally — sold out into the second, third, fourth weekend of the film’s run. So it definitely signals that The Odyssey will be around in theaters for quite a while, just like Oppenheimer was.” Director Christopher Nolan (left), with Matt Damon (as Odysseus) and Zendaya (as Athena), on the set of The Odyssey. Universal Pictures The appeal for younger audiences can be felt not just in ticket sales but through engagement in other ways, as social media memes embraced The Odyssey , which centers on Damon’s Odysseus making his arduous trip back to Ithaca after the Trojan War. Additionally, the film became the fastest to hit a million watches on Letterboxd by accomplishing the feat in six days, half the time that it took previous champ Barbie and other titles since, like Project Hail Mary and Backrooms . Notably, 2010’s Inception was the first feature to reach the seven-figure milestone, proving Nolan’s longstanding credibility with cinephiles. “Nolan has cultivated such an incredibly diverse audience, and a lot of it is that Gen Z and younger millennial side,” says Robbins. “That’s really all thanks to his library of films, especially over the last 10 or 15 years. We point to The Dark Knight and Inception and his earlier work, but also look at [2014’s] Interstellar . We saw high demand when Interstellar rereleased in theaters about a year and a half ago. It just kept gaining a following, and a lot of that was among younger moviegoers. With all of his films, there are tons of content creators breaking down his storytelling, his writing, maybe the cinematography or the music. There are just so many elements to his movies that intrigue and envelop people.” In recent years, studios have emphasized the importance of eventizing filmgoing, hoping for buzzy word of mouth to encourage audiences to experience a project in a way that they can’t from their couch, and premium large format screens have emerged as a key way to accomplish this. The word of mouth was clearly stellar, between the film’s A CinemaScore and a 94 percent approval rating from critics on Rotten Tomatoes, tied for Nolan’s best mark ever. This meant that viewers flocked not only to IMAX theaters — which pulled in $51.8 million globally to account for 23 percent of The Odyssey’ s haul — but especially to the 41 theaters worldwide that screen IMAX 70mm film. As for whether IMAX can expand the number of screens showing 70mm, Gelfond notes that the theater count is up from 30 at the time of the Oppenheimer release. “There are some inherent limitations on our ability to do it, including finding boxes that big to fit the right aspect ratio and finding projectors that we can refurbish,” he says. “We’re always looking, but it’s complicated.” The exec notes that he did see online conversation surrounding some individuals feeling priced out of the IMAX experience and adds about the company that does not set its ticket prices: “We’re as aggressive as we can be. We want to provide a great experience. We want the right aspect ratio. We want the projector to show the best image they can. We’re sensitive to the demand, but we’re not going to lower our quality standards.” After a bumpy stretch of July weekends that saw underperforming openings for such franchise entries as Supergirl , Minions & Monsters and Moana , the industry is quickly embracing the newfound optimism. And while Nolan may have stood out among his movie’s bold-faced names, there’s no doubt that the starry cast added to the spectacle. Among those sparking social media chatter for The Odyssey was Holland, whose role as Odysseus’ son, Telemachus, gave him the opportunity for a high-profile performance without his superhero suit (although he’ll be onscreen as Spidey soon enough, too). “His performance in The Odyssey shows a new side of him that will really just continue to grow his fan base,” observes Robbins. Perhaps Nolan himself best summed up a key element of his own movies’ appeal when recently explaining to THR about a goal that he carries throughout his work: “I try to leave each film I do with thematic concerns and questions that stay a little bit open for me — and hopefully the audience, too.” THR

title: ‘The Odyssey’ Posts Powerful $20M Tuesday; 2nd U.S. Weekend Could Top Christopher Nolan’s ‘Dark Knight’ Pics – Box Office
full_text: Skip to main content By Anthony D'Alessandro Anthony D'Alessandro Editorial Director/Box Office Editor @AwardsTony More Stories By Anthony ‘The Odyssey’ Posts Powerful $20M Tuesday; 2nd U.S. Weekend Could Top Christopher Nolan’s ‘Dark Knight’ Pics – Box Office Snoop Dogg Biopic From Universal Hits Theaters Next Summer Black Bear Hires Bianca Parkes As Head Of Marketing, Heather Phillips As Head Of Publicity View All July 21, 2026 4:42pm Matt Damon in 'The Odyssey' Universal The Odyssey is on its way to a $20M Tuesday, we understand, and if it gets past $20.86M today, it will become Christopher Nolan’s best Tuesday ever at the domestic B.O., ahead of 2008’s The Dark Knight. Currently, among all Tuesdays this year, the pic starring Matt Damon, Anne Hathaway, Robert Pattinson, Tom Holland, Zendaya, Jon Bernthal and Mia Goth is second behind Disney/Pixar’s Toy Story 5 ($23.69M, June 23). The Odyssey is currently second among all Tuesdays for a Universal movie behind Jurassic World ($24.3M, June 16, 2015). Related Stories Reviews ‘The Odyssey’ Review: Christopher Nolan Takes On Time, Myth And War In Epic Retelling News The 62 Movies That Have Made More Than $1 Billion At The Global Box Office The Odyssey will hold all Imax and PLF screens in its second weekend, charting a course for a 40%-50% ease in the $61.8M-$74M range. There’s a chance that the movie bests the second frame of Nolan’s Dark Knight movies, meaning either 2008’s The Dark Knight ($75.1M, -61%, the director’s highest-grossing sophomore frame) or 2012’s The Dark Knight Rises ($62.1M, -61% his second-highest). Superhero movies typically are front-loaded, which is reason why no one is expecting Odyssey to comp to the Dark Knight holds. Again, this is an by-appointment film with moviegoers eyeing the best seats, formats and showtimes. Oppenheimer, in a Barbie -dominated 2023 market, saw its second weekend dip by 43% with $46.7M.
